"Exactly," President Barry affirmed. "We need to ask them directly about their decision-making process. What intelligence led them to take such a drastic step? Was there an immediate threat that warranted a nuclear strike? And importantly, why there was no communication with us beforehand?"

The NSA advisor was already making notes. "I'll compile a list of questions for them. We also need to consider the possibility of Blackwatch operating with information that we are not privy to.

Barry nodded gravely. "That's a possibility. But it's our responsibility to ensure that any independent military force, especially one as powerful as Blackwatch, is acting in a manner consistent with our own national security policies."

Five hours later.

"Mr. President, we now have established a secure line of communication with Blackwatch," the NSA advisor reported as he returned to President Barry's office.

"Finally," President Barry sighed. "Patch them through."

The advisor nodded and activated the secure communication system. Richard's face appeared on the screen, looking both determined and weary.

"President Barry, this is Richard from Blackwatch," he began.

President Barry leaned forward. "Richard, we need answers. Why did Blackwatch launch a nuclear strike in Beijing without consulting us?"

"Mr. President, I understand your concerns. Our intel indicated that Lin Feng was using that location to summon and control hordes of zombies. The situation escalated rapidly, and we had to make an immediate decision. The threat he posed not just to the local area but potentially worldwide was too great to ignore…"

While Richard was explaining that, he was panting mid-sentence, showing visible signs of exhaustion. "We had intelligence suggesting Lin Feng had acquired new, powerful abilities that could have escalated the situation beyond control. Our primary focus was on neutralizing this immediate and unpredictable threat."

"Speaking of threats, are you dealing with one right now?" President Barry inquired.

"We are indeed. But it's under control, it's why I was able to return here in the first place," Richard explained.

"Well, you could have told us. I thought we were allies. Not telling us what you are about to do is not a good sign of a

healthy alliance," President Barry pointed out sternly.

Richard, rubbing his eyes as if tired of the conversation, responded, "Mr. President, with all due respect, in a situation like this, time is of the essence. If we had contacted you, we would have been bogged down with bureaucracy. That delay could have cost countless lives. Our decision to act swiftly was driven by the urgency of the threat posed by Lin Feng and his newfound capabilities."

President Barry's expression was a mix of understanding and concern. "I get the urgency, Richard, but you must understand that actions like these have significant geopolitical consequences. We need to be on the same page, especially when it involves a nuclear strike."

"Geopolitical? As far as I know, the United States government is the only functioning government in the world. I don't think China or Russia would file a complaint about our actions," Richard retorted.

"Seriously?" Barry leaned forward, his facial expression turning serious. "I thought you were a wise leader but I guess I was wrong."

"Look, Mr. President, I have been hunting Lin Feng since I was able to figure out what kind of monster he is while you and your colleagues are hiding deep beneath the mountain hoping that a better day would dawn. I've seen things that would make your worst nightmares seem like pleasant dreams. We operate in a world that has gone dark, where the usual rules don't apply.

Our mission is to ensure humanity's survival at any cost."

President Barry took a deep breath, trying to maintain his composure. "I understand, and that is our goal, but please next time, do inform us of your next move. If you told us, we wouldn't stop you, as long as it is justifiable. The nukes that you used could have resulted in human casualties hiding in Beijing."

"In war, it's expected that there would be human casualties," Richard firmly interrupted. "Our priority is to minimize those casualties, but sometimes difficult decisions have to be made. I apologize for not informing you beforehand, Mr. President, but I stand by the choice we made, and we are seeing the results of it.

The zombie hordes that we are dealing with right now have already been thinned, in two or three hours, we will clear it."

"I see…zombie hordes? We never faced that one before. But anyways, since you launched those nukes, do you have confirmation that Lin Feng is neutralized?"

"I'm going to conduct an operation to search for his remains," Richard replied. "But it's likely that Lin Feng is still alive, we have to assume the worst."

"In that case, I want my men on board as well," President Barry proposed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 295 - 295: Proposal Joint Operation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"On board?" Richard repeated. "Mr. President, are you suggesting a joint operation?"

"That is correct," President Barry affirmed. "Given the global implications of this threat, I believe it's essential we join forces for this operation. The USS Gerald R. Ford is currently positioned in the Pacific, and I can have a special forces team deployed to your location immediately."

"Uhm…Mr. President, with all due respect, I don't think your men will be up to the task. My men had been chasing Lin Feng and they know most of his tactics and strategies, your men will only slow them down. Though this may sound blunt to you, please, let us handle this one."

"Mr. Gonzales, you are underestimating my men. They are Navy Seals, the best of the best special forces in the world, they are up to any challenges thrown at them."

"But not the kind of challenges Lin Feng is throwing at us," Richard countered firmly. "I assure you, Mr. President, this isn't a matter of skill or bravery. Lin Feng and his forces operate in a manner unlike anything we've seen. Our men have adapted to this new reality in ways that are hard to comprehend for those not in the field."

President Barry's tone softened but remained firm. "I understand your concerns, Richard. However, this isn't just about combat effectiveness. It's about demonstrating a united front. The world needs to see that the United States and Blackwatch stand together against this threat. It sends a powerful message."

Richard sighed, realizing the political weight of the situation. "I see your point, Mr. President, and I agree on the need for unity. But we can't afford any missteps. If your team is coming, they need to be briefed extensively on the enemy we're facing. We can't have them learning on the fly; it's too risky."

"I assure you, Richard, they'll be fully briefed and ready," Barry responded confidently. "These men are professionals. They adapt quickly and will follow your command."

"Alright, Mr. President," Richard conceded.

"We'll integrate your team into our operation. But they must be prepared to follow our lead without hesitation."

"That's a given," Barry replied. "They will comply with your commands on the ground. You're the expert in this scenario, and they understand that."

"Good. Then we'll make the necessary arrangements," Richard said, a bit more reassured.

"Thank you, Richard," Barry said, a hint of relief in his voice. "I'll have my Chief of Staff coordinate with your team immediately. We're in this together."

"Yes, Mr. President, together," Richard repeated before ending the transmission. "Joint Operation…Well, they are Navy Seals indeed but they are only trained to fight humans, not supernatural entities?"

"But aren't we all trained to fight humans?" Mark chimed in.

"Yeah but your experience was accumulated by fighting non and mutated zombies, those Navy Seals didn't," Richard replied.

"Let's give them a chance," Richard continued, his tone shifting towards pragmatism. "We'll run a comprehensive briefing. They need to understand what we're up against – the unpredictability and sheer danger of these mutated zombies, and especially Lin Feng's capabilities."

Mark nodded in agreement. "Understood. We'll prepare the briefing materials. But we need to make it clear that this is no ordinary operation. The rules of engagement are different here."

"Exactly," Richard replied. "I'll personally oversee the integration. We need to ensure seamless communication and coordination. Any misalignment could be disastrous."

"On that note, we should establish clear command protocols," Mark suggested. "There can't be any confusion about who's calling the shots during critical moments."

"Agreed," Richard said. "I'll communicate our operational structure to the Navy Seals' commander. It's essential they understand that Blackwatch has the lead on this mission."

"Okay, with that matter settled, who are you going to send?" Mark asked.

"Isn't it obvious? Of course, Graves would lead the operation," Richard replied.

"I know it would be Graves but about the technology…are we going to tell them about it? Like they will surely question what Graves is wearing," Mark expressed his concern.

Mark's concern was valid. The advanced technology Blackwatch utilized, especially the Titan's Guard Mark II suits, was beyond anything the U.S. military had in its arsenal. The revelation could lead to a multitude of questions and potentially complicate the joint operation.

Richard pondered for a moment before responding. "You're right, Mark. The technology we have is something they've never seen. But transparency is crucial here. We can't have our allies in the dark, especially when they're fighting alongside us."

"Do you think they'll demand access to our technology?" Mark inquired, his brow furrowing.

"It's a possibility," Richard acknowledged. "But our priority is the mission at hand. We'll explain the technology to the extent necessary for operational effectiveness. Anything beyond that, we can discuss after the mission is completed."

"Hmm…but how do you address this issue then? Like how we are going to explain to them that we have this and they don't have that," Mark asked.

"It's simple, the United States I believe has agencies such as DARPA to develop futuristic technologies. We'll present our technology as a product of similar, albeit more advanced, research and development efforts. Hmm…let's add that we have been experimenting and studying it for over a decade and were able to make a functional prototype only recently," Richard elaborated.

Mark nodded. "That should satisfy their initial curiosity without revealing too much. But we should be prepared for more in-depth inquiries, especially after they see the capabilities of the Titan's Guard Mark II in action."

"True," Richard agreed. "For now, let's focus on the immediate concern—the zombies, how are their numbers?"

"Well…we have been fighting them for hours but the waves are still immense. All our armed forces are mobilizing to ensure that this will end in an hour or two, but I can already, this is going to be a huge mess, especially when clearing their corpses," Mark chuckled lightly, albeit with an undertone of weariness.

"Yeah, it's going to be a long day," Richard acknowledged, glancing at the monitors showing live feeds of the ongoing battle. "Once this is over, we'll need to regroup and debrief.

***

The wave persisted for another three hours with the Armed Forces of the Blackwatch doing everything they could to stop their advance, and little by little, the zombies' numbers increased, both in non and mutated. However, the notification Richard has been waiting for is not yet popping up.

"When is this wave going to end?"

As Richard said that, Sara flickered her finger, getting his attention.

"Sir…it appears that zombies in the NCR and those south of Clark are marching towards the north. It seems that they are heading this way," Sara reported.

"You gotta be kidding me, the whole population vs our forces? Give us a break."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 296 - 296: Ridiculous Mission
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

December 27th, 2023. Location unknown. The Seventh Naval Fleet of the United States Navy received a transmission from the Cheyenne Mountain Complex. It was decoded by the fleet's Chief Cryptologic Technician. He quickly deciphered the encrypted message.

The message, marked with the highest level of urgency, outlined a new directive from the President himself, requiring immediate action. Understanding its importance, he didn't waste a moment. He grabbed the printed transcript and made his way to the captain's quarters.

As he approached the captain's quarters, he knocked sharply on the door.

"Captain, urgent transmission from Cheyenne Mountain," he announced through the door.

The Captain of the Fleet opened the door swiftly. "What's the situation?"

Dawson handed him the transcript. "Sir, it's a directive involving a joint operation with Blackwatch. The President has ordered us to deploy a special forces team to assist in a mission against a significant threat."

"Blackwatch? Ahh…I have heard of them from the President. Well, let's see this."

There, the Captain read the detailed transcript. The document outlined the unprecedented threat posed by Lin Feng, an individual whom the United States learned was the one responsible for the apocalyptic world.

"I'll get the Navy Seals ready," Captain responded decisively after a brief pause.

***

The Captain walked down the long and narrow corridor of the ship, heading towards the briefing room where the Navy Seals team was assembled. As he entered the room, all eyes turned towards him.

"Listen up," the Captain began. "We've received direct orders from the President for a joint operation with a paramilitary group known as Blackwatch. Our primary objective is to confirm the death of an individual named Lin Feng in China. He's been identified as a major threat, responsible for the current state of the world."

There was a slight stir among the Seals; they were seasoned soldiers but were not used to collaborating with outside groups, especially on foreign soil.

"The operation will take place in the Philippines, at New Clark City. That's Blackwatch's base of operations. Once we land, Blackwatch operatives will debrief us with the mission specifics," the Captain continued.

One of the Seals raised his hand. "Sir, what's the nature of this Lin Feng? And why are we working with Blackwatch?"

The Captain nodded, anticipating these questions. "Lin Feng is no ordinary enemy. He's believed to possess abilities that have led to the creation of mutated zombies and other threats we can't yet fully understand. As for Blackwatch, they've been on the front lines of this fight. They have vital intel and experience that we need."

Another Seal chimed in, "Sir, will we be operating under Blackwatch command?"

"Yes," the Captain confirmed. "For this operation, we follow their lead. They have the home-field advantage and know more about what we're dealing with."

Another Seal scoffed. "Who the heck is Blackwatch? And why are we working under them? Shouldn't it be the other way around?"

"I understand the feelings," the Captain responded calmly, addressing the skeptical Seal. "Blackwatch is a highly specialized paramilitary force that has been combating this specific threat for some time now. They have experience and knowledge about Lin Feng and these mutated threats that we don't. It's crucial we utilize their expertise."

After hearing that, the Captain of the Navy Seals stood up. "Sir, with all due respect, this doesn't sit right with us. Blackwatch? A group that most of us have never even heard of, and now we're supposed to follow their lead on foreign soil? It's unprecedented."

The room grew tense as several other Seals nodded in agreement, standing up to join their Captain. It was clear that the idea of being commanded by an unknown paramilitary group did not sit well with these elite soldiers.

The Captain of the Fleet, sensing the growing dissent, raised his hand to command attention. "Gentlemen, I understand your concerns, but are we really considering disobeying direct orders from the President? Are we willing to compromise the mission because we think we are the superior force?"

The room fell silent. The Captain's words resonated with the Seals, reminding them of their duty and the state of the world they are in.

The Captain of the Navy Seals, after a moment of contemplation, spoke again, "It's not about superiority, sir. It's about protocol and working with an entity we know nothing about. How can we ensure operational success under these conditions?"

The Fleet Captain responded firmly, "I hear you, but these are extraordinary circumstances. The President himself has given this directive. Our job is to follow orders and ensure the mission's success. Blackwatch has been fighting this threat directly, that should be enough."

After a brief pause, he continued, "You are the best of the best, trained to adapt and overcome any challenge. This is no different. We must trust the chain of command and the decisions made at the highest level. This mission is about more than just our protocol – it's about the future of our world."

The room was quiet, the Seals contemplating the weight of their responsibility. Finally, the Captain of the Navy Seals sighed and nodded, signaling his acceptance. "Alright, sir. We'll follow the orders. We trust the President's judgment. But we'll need full intel on Blackwatch and its operational methods.

Our team's effectiveness depends on it."

"You'll get it once you land in the Philippines," The Captain of the Fleet said. "Reynolds, behave yourself there okay? If you want the world to return to normal, that is your chance to make it happen."

"Understood, sir," Captain Reynolds of the Navy Seals replied. "Now, how will we get there?"

"A Grumman C-2 Greyhound transport aircraft is being readied for you," the Fleet Captain informed. "It'll take off from the USS Gerald R. Ford and deliver you to Clark International Airport in the Philippines. From there, transportation will be arranged by Blackwatch."

Captain Reynolds nodded. "We'll prepare for our immediate departure then. Gear and weapons check will be done en route."

"Don't let me down," the Fleet Captain added, his tone serious yet encouraging.

"We won't, sir," Reynolds assured him.

The Fleet Captain watched as Captain Reynolds and his men quickly left the briefing room. Within moments, the Navy Seals were gathered on the deck of the USS Gerald R. Ford, systematically checking their gear.

Once all checks were completed, the team made their way to the Grumman C-2 Greyhound.

As the Navy Seals boarded the aircraft, Captain Reynolds took a moment to look back at the massive vessel that had been their home for almost four months, he wondered if he was going to return here safely.

The aircraft's engines roared to life, signaling the start of their journey. Captain Reynolds settled into his seat and prepared for whatever was to come.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 297 - 297: The Arrival of the Navy SEALs in the Philippines
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Reynolds was jolted awake by the roar of engines, a sound that was both familiar and imposing. Through the small window of the Greyhound, he caught sight of two fighter aircraft escorting them, their sleek forms cutting through the sky with ease. One, unmistakably an F-35, matched their speed on the right.

"Unidentified flight, this is Lightning 0-1, you are entering Blackwatch airspace," came a crisp voice over the comms, authoritative and clear. The call sign 'Lightning 0-1' referred to the F-35 on their right, a call sign that Reynolds recognized as part of the escort protocol.

"Pilgrim 1-1 to Lightning 0-1, we read you loud and clear. We're the Greyhound, carrying Navy Seals for the joint operation. Awaiting instructions," the Greyhound's pilot responded.

"Copy that, Pilgrim 1-1. Maintain your current altitude and speed. Escort formation will guide you to the runway. Welcome to Clark International Airport," Lightning 0-1 replied, the professionalism in the pilot's tone doing little to ease the tension that had built up in the cabin.

As they neared the runway, the F-35s broke formation with a graceful maneuver that spoke volumes of the pilots' skill. Reynolds watched as the fighters peeled away, their engines a distant thunder as they disappeared from view.

`

"Is that an F-35, since when did the Philippines have possession of it?" One of the Navy Seals asked.

"They are not from the Philippine Armed Forces, they are from Blackwatch, a secretive private military firm. However, it baffles me how a private military company was able to avail the F-35s. Isn't the sale of the F-35s exclusive to the United States partner's armed forces?" Reynolds commented.

"Yeah, it's unusual. Blackwatch must have significant backing and connections to operate on this level," another Seal chimed in, his tone a mix of curiosity and concern.

Meanwhile, the pilot of the Greyhound began communicating with the air tower of the Clark Airport.

"Pilgrim 1-1 to Clark Tower, requesting vector for final approach, over," the Greyhound's pilot initiated the landing protocol.

"Clark Tower to Pilgrim 1-1, you are cleared for vector 270 to runway 3. Wind speed at 5 knots, southeast. Altimeter setting 1012, maintain flight level until established on the localizer, over," the air traffic controller responded, providing precise details for a safe approach.

"Pilgrim 1-1 acknowledges vector 270 to runway 3, maintaining until localizer capture," the pilot confirmed, adjusting the aircraft's trajectory. It aligned with the designated runway, its sophisticated navigation systems locking onto the guidance signals emitted by the airport's instrumentation.

As the Greyhound descended, the pilot smoothly engaged the ILS (Instrument Landing System), a critical aid that provides lateral and vertical guidance necessary for a precision landing. "Flaps to 30, gear down," the co-pilot announced, executing part of the landing checklist that would ensure the aircraft's aerodynamic configuration was optimized for touchdown.

The runway lights became visible, a welcoming sight that marked the end of their journey and the beginning of their mission on Philippine soil. "Pilgrim 1-1 on final for runway 3," the pilot reported.

"Runway 3 is yours, Pilgrim 1-1. Blackwatch has been notified of your arrival. Tower out," the controller gave their final instruction, signaling the successful end of their communication.

The Greyhound touched down with practiced ease, its landing gear absorbing the impact as it decelerated along the runway, ready to taxi to the assigned area where Blackwatch operatives awaited.

Inside the Greyhound.

"Okay boys, we have arrived at the Blackwatch Base of Operations. No,w I don't want any hiccups in the interaction. If you have something to say and you know it's not going to come out good, keep it to yourself. I'll do all the talking, you watch my back. Is that understood?"

"Yes, sir," the team responded in unison. Each member of the team checked their gear one last time, ensuring that everything was in place and accessible. They understood the gravity of the situation and the importance of maintaining professionalism, especially when dealing with an entity as enigmatic as Blackwatch.

The side door of the Greyhound opened, and Reynolds was the first to step out, his boots hitting the tarmac with a determined thud. He was met by the sight of a small contingent of Blackwatch operatives, easily identifiable by their distinctive uniforms and the calm, collected manner in which they stood waiting.

Leading the Blackwatch welcoming party was a man who carried himself with an air of authority. He approached Reynolds and extended his hand. "Captain Reynolds, I presume? I'm Commander Graves, Special Operations, Blackwatch. We've been expecting you."

Reynolds shook his hand, noting the firm grip. "Commander Graves, it's a pleasure. As instructed, we're here to collaborate on the operation. My team is ready to receive the mission briefing."

Grayson nodded, gesturing for Reynolds and his team to follow him toward a series of black SUVs parked nearby. "We'll brief you fully at our command center. Time is of the essence, so we'll proceed directly there."

As they walked towards the vehicles, Reynolds glanced back at his team, giving them a nod that conveyed a wealth of meaning—stay alert, stay focused, and follow his lead. The Seals fell into step behind him, their training kicking in as they prepared themselves mentally for the briefing and the mission that lay ahead.

While heading to the vehicle, Reynolds and his team glanced around and noted the aircraft that is on the airport, military aircraft, mostly American, were either parked or flying. They even have Apaches and the A-10s. But—he also noticed that it was rather noisy.

"Are you perhaps in the middle of the operation?" Reynolds asked Graves.

Graves nodded, not batting an eye at him. "We are dealing with a zombie horde that has been pestering us for days. Reports indicate that the zombies from the southern region are heading up here, it's like they are being mind-controlled."

"Let me assume, it's Lin Feng, right? The Chinese guy?"

Graves nodded. "Yeah…Lin Feng. There is a high chance that he is still alive after the nuclear strike, because if he didn't survive that, then those zombies would have stopped heading to our base."

Graves opened the door and gestured for Reynolds and his team to get into the SUVs. "Let's discuss the details en route to the command center. We've got a lot to cover, and time is not on our side."

As the team settled into the vehicles, Graves continued, "Our intelligence suggests Lin Feng is using some form of enhanced telepathic control over the zombies. Through what method? It's still uncertain."

"I'll know more of it once we arrive in the command center, so let's save the talk for later," Reynolds said and continued. "Anyways, I'm curious. Have you worked in the US? Special Forces?"

Graves shook his head. "No."

Graves was the first to step out, gesturing for Reynolds and his team to follow. The command center building of Blackwatch stood imposingly before them. As they approached, Reynolds took note of the efficiency and discipline that seemed to permeate the air around Blackwatch operatives. Despite the non-standard nature of their operations, everything about them screamed military precision.

Inside. Personnel moved with purpose, their focus undivided as they monitored screens, communicated over comms, and coordinated operations. Graves led the Navy Seals through a series of corridors until they reached a secure door. After a brief security check, they were admitted into a spacious, well-equipped briefing room.

"Please, have a seat," Graves instructed, directing them towards a large table surrounded by high-back chairs. The walls were lined with digital displays, showing real-time satellite imagery, troop movements, and data streams that Reynolds couldn't immediately decipher. It was clear that Blackwatch had access to resources and intelligence that rivaled that of nation-states.

A few moments later, the door opened again, and a young man entered the room. His presence was unassuming, and his attire, while neat, was far from what Reynolds expected of someone in charge of such an operation. He was in his early twenties, with an alertness in his eyes that belied his youthful appearance. This, Reynolds realized, was Richard Gonzales, the CEO of Blackwatch.

"Welcome, gentlemen," Richard began. "I'm Richard Gonzales. I understand you've had quite the journey to get here."

Reynolds, taken aback by Richard's age, took a moment before responding.

"Captain Reynolds, sir. We appreciate the hospitality. We were briefed on the situation en route, but we're looking forward to understanding more about our role in this operation."

Richard nodded, a slight smile crossing his features. "Of course. Let's get straight to it then."

He turned to a console, initiating a series of images that flickered to life on the displays around them. "As you're aware, our primary objective is Lin Feng. His ability to control the infected has escalated the threat level significantly."

Richard detailed the intelligence gathered on Lin Feng, including satellite imagery, previous footage about him, and firsthand accounts from Blackwatch operatives who had encountered his forces. Reynolds listened intently, his initial surprise at Richard's youthfulness giving way to respect for his command of the situation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 298 - 298: Drawing a Line
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Regarding travel to China, given the limitations of the Greyhound's range, how are we planning to approach this?" Captain Reynolds asked, looking for a straightforward logistical solution.

Richard explained concisely, "We'll use an A400M Airbus, designed for long-range and high-altitude missions. It's capable of performing HALO jumps, which will be crucial for our insertion into the area of operations."

"A400M Airbus? So you even have those as well huh?" Reynolds mused, seemingly amused at the arsenal Blackwatch has.

"We have everything we need," Richard simply replied. "Well, you guys are familiar with the HALO jump right?"

"Absolutely," Captain Reynolds responded without hesitation. "HALO jumps are part of our standard training. We're fully qualified for that type of insertion."

Richard nodded, satisfied with the answer. "Good. We'll schedule the jump for 2000 hours tomorrow. The A400M will take off from Clark Air Base. You'll be jumping under the cover of night, minimizing visibility to enemy radar and ground forces."

Reynolds took a moment to process the details, then turned to address his team. "You heard the plan. Let's make sure we're all prepped. Review your HALO protocols and gear requirements tomorrow evening. We need to be sharp."

Richard added, "I'll provide you with all the necessary intel and maps of the landing zone. The objective is to land undetected within a 2-kilometer radius of Lin Feng's last known location. From there, you'll trek to the target area. Stealth is paramount; we can't afford any alerts."

"Got it," Reynolds affirmed, already running through the mental checklist of what his team would need. "Anyways, since this is going to be a joint operation, how many of your guys are going to come with us?"

"That would be that gentleman over there," Richard tilted his head to where Graves was standing.

Graves raised a hand and waved, acknowledging the question. "We'll be joining you with ten of our special forces operatives, making the team twenty-strong," he informed them.

Reynolds nodded, processing the numbers. "Twenty. A good size for mobility and maintaining a low profile. Your team, I assume, has extensive experience with this kind of operation?"

"Absolutely," Graves confirmed. "Every one of my team members has been in multiple engagements against similar threats. We're well-versed in unconventional warfare and have operated in high-threat environments."

Richard chimed in, "The composition of our joint team is designed to maximize our operational capabilities. Your expertise combined with our intel and local knowledge should give us the upper hand."

Reynolds looked over at Graves and his team, assessing them with a professional eye. They were a diverse group, each with a demeanor that spoke of experience and readiness. "Before we leave, I'm going to need to watch all the videos you have on Lin Feng. We want to know how that monster fights."

"No problem, we will provide it to your room," Richard replied. "And if you have any specific preparations or requests, let us know. We want to ensure you have everything you need for the operation."

"Thanks," Reynolds said, feeling a bit more reassured by the support. "We'll also need to sync our communication gear to ensure compatibility. The last thing we need is a comm failure in the field."

Graves nodded in agreement. "We'll handle that first thing in the morning. Our techs will work with yours to make sure everything's interoperable."

"Is there anything else we need to be aware of about anything?"

"Oh, there is one," Richard said, thinking that this was the perfect time to bring that up. "Graves, can you show it to them?"

"With pleasure," Graves walked forward and with a tap on his chest, the Titan's Guard Mark II materialized, wrapping and coiling around his body as the nanotechnology activated. The suit, sleek and evidently advanced, clung to Graves like a second skin, enhancing his silhouette with an almost futuristic aura.

Reynolds and his team looked on, a mix of awe and surprise evident on their faces. The technology was unlike anything they had seen, even in their extensive training and operations.

"All of our special forces will be equipped with this suit for the operation," Richard informed them, noting their intrigued expressions. "It offers enhanced protection, agility, and a variety of integrated combat systems."

Reynolds, still eyeing the suit, asked the question on everyone's mind, "Will we have access to these suits as well?"

Richard shook his head, a slightly apologetic tone in his voice. "I'm afraid not. The Titan's Guard Mark II is exclusive to Blackwatch personnel. It requires extensive training and practice to master its technology. It's not something that can be used effectively without that background."

"But how were these suits developed?" Reynolds inquired, curiosity piqued by the blend of advanced materials and technology.

"That's classified, I'm afraid," Richard responded with a firm yet polite tone. "What I can share is that it's the culmination of years of research and development, incorporating both existing military tech and advancements that our R&D teams have achieved."

"Well, if we are fighting the same threat that wants the extinction of humanity, wouldn't it be best to share the technology with us?"

"I agree with your logic but this suit is what gives us the edge. We are going to defeat Lin Feng, and when we do, the world will return to normal and it's in everyone's self-interest to maintain certain advantages. Plus, the technology embedded in these suits is highly proprietary and sensitive. Sharing it isn't as straightforward as it might seem," Richard explained.

Reynolds, while not fully satisfied with the answer, understood the complexities involved. "Understood. Operational security and technological edge are critical."

"Exactly," Richard said, eager to steer the conversation back to the mission at hand. "Let's focus on the operation. Our priority is to ensure that we are all prepared and on the same page. The success of this mission could very well hinge on our ability to work seamlessly as a unit. After all, the United States is an ally of Blackwatch.

We did things without informing you and now we are making up for it by letting you join this operation."

"Ahh…that's how you see that huh?" Reynolds mused and continued. "Right. Let's get down to brass tacks then. You've got the tech and the intel, we've got the training and experience. Together, we'll make this work."

Richard smiled slightly. "I have no doubt. Now, if there are no further questions, I suggest we all get some rest. Tomorrow will be a long day."

Reynolds agreed, signaling to his team that the meeting was over. "Alright, team, let's get ready. Review everything once more, check your gear, and then get some shut-eye. We move at 2000 hours."

As the team dispersed, Reynolds lingered for a moment, his gaze falling once again on the Titan's Guard Mark II suit. Once he witnesses its capabilities, he'll report it back to the president.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 299 - 299: Touchdown to Beijing
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A day later, seven thirty in the evening. Richard was in the command center, having a hearty dinner with his little sister, Lisa.

"Brother—I heard you are going to conduct another operation, will this be the end?" Lisa asked with a hopeful tone.

Richard glanced at Lisa, putting his spoon down. "Well the operation is to confirm whether Lin Feng is dead or alive, I hope it's the former because I really want this nightmare to end."

If there is a way for Richard to know that they got Lin Feng, for example, a notification informing him that they killed Lin Feng like killing the boss, it would have been convenient. But there was no notification, so they had to confirm it in the old-fashioned way—through reconnaissance and confirmation on the ground.

"Are you coming with them?"

Richard shook his head. "If I am, I will not be having this dinner together with you. I'll be overseeing the operation from here, coordinating with the teams on the ground to ensure they have everything they need for the mission's success."

Lisa heaved a sigh of relief. "That's more reassuring. But how about the zombies outside the camp? The residents are getting nervous and scared due to the constant sound of gunfire and explosions every night."

Richard paused, understanding the concern in his sister's voice. "We've increased patrols around the perimeter and reinforced our defenses. The zombies pose a threat, but we're holding them back. Our main focus, however, remains on taking down Lin Feng. Without him, the zombies will eventually become less of a coordinated threat."

Lisa nodded, looking somewhat reassured but still worried. "I just hope everything goes back to how it used to be."

"We all do," Richard replied, offering a comforting smile. "But until then, we have to stay strong and do what we can to protect each other."

Moments later, Richard finished his meal and bid farewell to his little sister. After that, he went to the command center where the staffers saluted as he entered.

"At ease, gentlemen," Richard said, waving his hand dismissively.

Mark stepped forward from the group of staffers, approaching Richard.

"Sir, Graves and the Navy Seals are on their way to the tarmac. The A400M is prepped and ready for the HALO jump."

Richard nodded, his expression serious. "Good. Make sure they have all the intel and gear they need. This mission doesn't allow for any mistakes."

"Absolutely, sir. We've double-checked everything. The team is equipped with the latest intel on Lin Feng's last known location and the surrounding area. They're ready," Mark assured him.

Richard's gaze lingered on Mark, conveying his trust in his team's preparation. "Keep me updated on their status. Once they're airborne, I want regular check-ins."

"Will do, sir. We've also set up a secure line for real-time updates. You'll have eyes and ears on the operation from start to finish," Mark added, indicating the level of communication and surveillance they had arranged for this critical mission.

"Excellent. And Mark, make sure the defense perimeter is on high alert while we're focused on this operation. We can't afford any distractions," Richard instructed. After all, the wave hasn't ended yet and there are still waves of zombies heading towards the camp.

***

Meanwhile, on the tarmac, 20 personnel walked towards the A400M Airbus. Graves led the group, his gaze fixed ahead, while Captain Reynolds and his Navy Seals followed, each member carrying their gear with practiced ease.

As they approached the aircraft, the pilot and co-pilot conducted final checks, ensuring the Airbus was ready for the mission ahead. The ground crew gave a thumbs-up, signaling that everything was set for departure.

Graves turned to Reynolds and his team, his expression grave. "Remember, once we're in the air, there's no turning back. We drop in, complete the mission, and extract."

Reynolds nodded, his face set in determination. "We're ready. Let's end this."

One by one, the team boarded the Airbus, their gear stowed efficiently. The interior of the aircraft was dimly lit, the only sound was the hum of the engines as they powered up. The pilot's voice came over the intercom, "All personnel, strap in. We're taking off in five minutes."

As the aircraft began its taxi to the runway, Richard's voice came through the secure line. "All stations, this is Eagle Actual. You're about to embark on a critical mission. I have every confidence in you. Stay sharp, stay focused, and come back safely."

The co-pilot's voice came over the intercom once more, "Tower, this is A400M Airbus, call sign Falcon 0-2, requesting clearance for takeoff."

"Falcon 0-2, this is Clark Air Tower. You are cleared for takeoff. Proceed to runway three-zero-left and await further instructions."

The pilot acknowledged the clearance, "Clark Air Tower, Falcon 0-2 copies. Heading to three-zero-left, standing by."

As the Airbus taxied into position on the runway, the controller provided final instructions, "Falcon 0-2, you are clear for takeoff. Good luck and Godspeed."

With that, the pilot pushed the throttle forward, and the massive aircraft began its acceleration down the runway. The A400M engines roared to life, generating the necessary thrust for takeoff.

The flight from New Clark City to Beijing is approximately seven hours. That means the team will arrive in Beijing at 0300 HRS. Where the undead or zombies are inactive due to the lack of stimuli.

And in those seven hours, the team remained silent, as if silently preparing for their mission ahead. Some Blackwatch Special Forces were mouthing a prayer. They were aware of the capabilities of Lin Feng on how he easily defeated and overwhelmed them in the first encounter. It goes the same for the Navy Seals who have watched the video of Lin Feng's fighting style.

Reynolds even compared it to Superman, as Lin Feng exhibited supernatural strength and speed, and watched how they easily defeated Blackwatch in such a fashion.

Seven hours later, the pilot of the A400M Airbus spoke through the intercom.

"Wake up, Specters! We are now entering Beijing's Airspace."

Graves and Reynolds rose to their feet and peered through the small window, offering them a view of Beijing below.

"Damn…you really nuked the capital of China huh?" Reynolds commented.

"We don't have a choice, if we don't strike him, the numbers of zombies will increase dramatically to the point of overwhelming the defense of the camp," Graves said and continued. "Gear up, prepare your men to jump."

***

Down below Beijing.

"Master…it seems that we have visitors," Su Xue said, gazing across the night sky.

"Yeah…let's prepare a grand reception for them," Lin Feng smirked.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 300 - 300: Beijing Part 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The team of 20, including Blackwatch special forces and Navy Seals, commenced their HALO jump over Beijing just past midnight. They jumped from the A400M Airbus at high altitudes, each equipped with oxygen masks due to the thin air and low temperatures at the jump height. Their objective was to land discreetly within a 2-kilometer radius of their target area, where Lin Feng was last reported to be.

As they descended towards their landing zone, the operatives relied on GPS devices to navigate through the night, ensuring accuracy despite the limited visibility. The jump, executed at 25,000 feet, required them to deploy their parachutes at around 3,500 feet to safely reach the ground.

Upon landing, the team quickly regrouped, checking their equipment and confirming their positions. The city below was largely quiet, with minimal light, reflecting the extensive damage and abandonment resulting from ongoing conflicts.

"Shit…this is my first time setting foot in a city that has been blasted by a nuclear bomb," Reynolds commented.

"Fortunately, the nuke was detonated in the air, so radiation is minimal to the point it won't affect us for the duration of the operation," Graves added, providing some reassurance to the team. "Let's keep moving. Time is critical."

The group, now fully assembled, began their trek towards the designated target location. They moved in a tight formation, with Blackwatch operatives leading the way, utilizing their navigation tools. As they walked, Graves and Reynolds had a chit-chat.

"So…this is your first time in the field prior to the apocalypse right?" Graves asked.

"Yes," Reynolds nodded his head.

"Where were you at that time?" Graves added.

"I'm in Hawaii, with my girlfriend. I just proposed to her in a day before the zombie apocalypse. Luckily I was able to evacuate her into the safe zone in Hawaii, and she's been there ever since. I promised to her that one day, we are going to marry in a world where this chaos has ended."

Hearing that, Graves felt a sense of empathy towards Reynolds. "That's a promise worth fighting for," Graves responded, his voice carrying a tone of respect. "We all have something or someone that keeps us going through this nightmare. Let's make sure we all get back to fulfill those promises."

"How about you? Where were you when the apocalypse started?" Reynolds asked.

"I'm in the Philippines, having a moment with my girlfriend," Graves said, lying through his teeth. He couldn't tell him that he was summoned by Richards.

"Oh…you also have a girlfriend huh? You promised her that you'll be back?"

Graves nodded, maintaining his composed facade. "Yes, something like that," he replied, opting for a vague answer to steer the conversation away from personal details.

The conversation was cut short as they neared their objective. The ruins of the city loomed larger as they advanced, the destruction more apparent with each step. Buildings that once stood proud were now shells of their former selves, windows blown out, facades crumbled.

Suddenly—they heard a screeching sound.

"Contact at ten o'clock!"

One of the Navy Seals called out and when Graves and Richard looked in that direction, they saw a Hunter leaping towards their position.

Graves activated his suit, and Reynolds and the rest of the Navy Seals opened fire. The Hunter faltered the moment the bullets landed on its body, and Graves took the moment of vulnerability as he armed his plasma blade, and then chopped the Hunter's head.

After dealing with the Hunter, the situation escalated quickly as a horde of zombies, attracted by the noise, began rushing towards their position. The team, already on high alert, prepared for the onslaught. The Blackwatch Special Forces, equipped with their Titan's Guard suits, stepped forward, their advanced armor lighting up as they activated various defensive and offensive systems.

Reynolds and the Navy Seals, though without the advanced technology of the Titan's Guard, were no less ready. They formed a defensive line, rifles aimed at the approaching undead. The sound of gunfire filled the air as they opened fire, taking down the zombies with precise, disciplined shots.

Graves showcased the capabilities of the Titan's Guard suits. With enhanced strength and speed, they moved through the zombies, their weapons—equipped directly into the suits—mowing down the undead. Despite the intensity of the encounter, the team worked seamlessly together, covering each other's backs and efficiently clearing the area of the threat.

The zombies, no match for the combined might of the Blackwatch and Navy Seals, were quickly neutralized, leaving the area once again quiet but for the echo of the last shots fired.

Breathing heavily from the exertion, Reynolds looked over at Graves and the Blackwatch operatives, a newfound respect in his eyes. "Impressive gear," he remarked, nodding towards the Titan's Guard suits.

Graves, deactivating his suit's offensive mode, gave a short nod in acknowledgment. "It gets the job done," he replied. "But let's not linger. We've made enough noise as it is. Lin Feng or his watchers could be aware of our presence now."

"We are already aware," a feminine voice reverberated in the field. The Blackwatch and Navy Seals all snapped their heads towards the source of the voice, and there they saw a woman that is familiar to the Blackwatch.

"Seo-Jun," Graves said.

"Who is that?" Reynolds asked.

"She is an associate of Lin Feng, formidable and very dangerous," Graves replied. "She is going to be a pain in the ass. Her abilities are these pinkish thorns that erase matter and materialize it in its corrupted form. For example, she can erase a missile and materialize it and control it under her will."

Reynolds, taking the warning seriously, signaled his team to prepare for combat. "What's the plan?" he asked Graves, looking for guidance on how to engage a foe they had little experience with.

Graves pondered for a moment, aware that a direct confrontation might not be in their best interest. "We need to avoid her thorns at all costs. Blackwatch, engage with ranged weapons. Navy Seals, provide covering fire and be ready to move on my command."

As Seo-Jun began to advance, her powers became evident. The ground around her feet started to distort, with pinkish thorns sprouting and aiming towards the team. The Blackwatch operatives, relying on their suits' agility, dodged the initial volley, returning fire with their integrated weapons.

Reynolds and the Navy Seals, though lacking the technological advantage, compensated with their training and precision. They fired in controlled bursts, aiming to distract and pin down Seo-Jun without directly engaging her deadly abilities.

However—the pinkish thorns were tough, their M4 Carbines were simply bouncing off or being absorbed by the thorns, rendering their efforts ineffective.

"Shit…things really have changed huh?" Reynolds observed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 301 - 301: Beijing Part 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As the situation with Seo-Jun escalated, Reynolds realized that their conventional tactics were ineffective against her powers. The pinkish thorns not only absorbed their bullets but also began to coil around, creating a barrier between them and Seo-Jun, limiting their movement and strategic options.

"Bring out the javelin missile," Reynolds instructed one of the Navy Seals.

The Seals who were carrying the Javelin nodded in acknowledgment. He prepared the Javelin, aimed it at Seo-Jun, and pulled the trigger.

The missile, with its powerful propulsion, shot out towards Seo-Jun at incredible speed. However, Seo-Jun, with a calm demeanor, extended her hand towards the incoming missile. The pinkish thorns surged forward, meeting the missile mid-air. They surrounded it, erasing it as if gnawing through it. Moments later, the missile was gone, leaving no trace behind.

With a flick of her finger, Seo-Jun's pinkish thorns rebuilt the missile that was erased. The reconstituted missile, now under her control, turned around and hounded towards the Navy Seals. The operatives, realizing the impending threat, scrambled for cover.

"Scatter!" Graves shouted, pushing Reynolds out of the missile's trajectory. The Blackwatch operatives, utilizing the agility provided by their Titan's Guard suits, managed to move out of harm's way just as the missile exploded where they had been moments before.

The explosion sent shockwaves through the area, dust and debris flying into the air. As the dust settled, Reynolds and Graves regrouped, assessing their team for injuries. Thankfully, due to their quick reactions, there were no casualties.

"That was close," Reynolds gasped, brushing off the dust from his uniform. "So that's what you are telling me about."

"Yeah…told you, she can erase matter and reconstruct it under her control," Graves confirmed, his eyes fixed on Seo-Jun. "Let our men handle this. Conventional weapons won't work here."

"Okay…it's all yours," Reynolds said.

The Blackwatch operatives, encased in their Titan's Guard Mark II suits, took to the air with the thrusters on their backs, encircling Seo-Jun from all directions. The night sky was momentarily illuminated by the glow of the plasma cannons charging up, casting an eerie light over the ruined cityscape.

Seo-Jun, undeterred by the show of force, prepared her thorns for another assault. However, the operatives had the advantage of aerial mobility, dodging her attacks with swift maneuvers. The plasma cannons unleashed their fury, sending concentrated beams of energy toward her from multiple angles.

Seo-Jun's thorn shield struggled under the onslaught, the pinkish barrier flickering under the intensity of the plasma.

Graves, observing the weakening of her defenses, seized the opportunity for a decisive strike.

"Cover me," he communicated to the team, his suit's thrusters propelling him forward at high speed. The other operatives intensified their barrage, drawing Seo-Jun's focus as Graves prepared his final move.

With precision timing, as the plasma cannons created an opening in Seo-Jun's defenses, Graves activated his suit's most powerful weapon—a high-intensity plasma beam. The beam, glowing with almost blinding brightness, shot forth from Graves' arm cannon, piercing through the night and hitting Seo-Jun directly.

The impact was catastrophic. Seo-Jun's attempt to counter with her thorns was futile; the plasma beam's energy was overwhelming. In a matter of seconds, the beam obliterated her very being, leaving behind a fading glow where she once stood.

The Blackwatch operatives landed, their suits powering down as the immediate threat was neutralized. Reynolds, along with the Navy Seals, looked on in awe and relief.

"Did you see that?"

"Yeah…they have plasma beams and cannons…this is some high-tech shit! Even DARPA is decades away from developing that kind of technology," one of the Navy Seals muttered, shaking his head in disbelief.

Graves, overhearing the conversation, merely nodded. "The Titan's Guard suits are the pinnacle of Blackwatch's technology. They're designed for situations exactly like this one."

Reynolds glanced at the spot where Seo-Jun had been just moments ago, now nothing more than scorched earth. "I can't believe it's over just like that. She seemed... invincible."

Graves looked around at the quiet city, the aftermath of their confrontation settling in. "It's never that simple. Seo-Jun was tough, but Lin Feng is the real threat. I think he will know that we killed one of her associates. I can still remember whenever we kill her associates, the others would be enraged to the point they'll want to kill us so badly."

"Look Graves…at this point, we don't think we have the right weapons to counter this threat," Reynolds said. "If our weapons can't even scratch their defenses, what chance do we have?"

"I understand your concern, but the reason why we are here is to confirm if Lin Feng is alive or not. And it seems that he is alive."

"We can't return with a seems, we need evidence that he is alive," Reynolds said.

"Yeah…but dead men tell no tales. Look, any moment now, Lin Feng's associates or Lin Feng himself would show up and kill us," Graves warned. "This is all the confirmation we need. Lin Feng is like a god to his associates. If Lin Feng really died from that strike, they'd be shouting and cursing at us. But she didn't.

I'm calling an Eagle."

"Specter-1 to Eagle Actual," Graves said.

"This is Eagle Actual, send traffic."

"Actual, we have eliminated one of Lin Feng's associates, specifically Seo-Jun. We are heading out now as we may believe that Lin Feng is still alive," Graves responded.

"We may believe?" Richard repeated. "How certain are you that Lin Feng is still alive based on this encounter?"

Graves took a moment before responding, "Based on the behavior of Seo-Jun and the intensity of the encounter, it's highly likely Lin Feng is still operational. If he were not, we'd see a different reaction from his associates

"Understood, Specter-1. Proceed with caution and extract as soon as possible. We'll prepare for the next phase based on your assessment. Eagle Actual out."

After the communication ended, Reynolds looked at Graves with a mixture of frustration and resignation. "So, what's our exit strategy? We're deep in enemy territory, and if Lin Feng is alive, we're walking into a hornet's nest."

Graves scanned the desolate landscape, his mind racing for solutions. "We move fast and silent. We came in undetected; we left the same way. Blackwatch will cover the retreat while Navy Seals secure our six. We have the tech and the training; we use it to our advantage."

The team quickly regrouped their brief respite over. However—.

"Going somewhere?"

A chill ran down Lin Feng's spine, he definitely heard that voice before and there was no mistaking it.

"It's Lin Feng," Graves announced.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 302 - 302: It's Lin Feng
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"You won't get out of this alive," Lin Feng's voice boomed from behind. Graves glanced over his shoulder and saw Lin Feng hovering in the sky, its black wings flapping behind his back. Down below are his associates, who were baring their teeth, as if angered by the loss of their comrades.

"Reynolds…this is the confirmation we need that Lin Feng is alive."

"Looks like my promise to my girlfriend won't come true huh?" Reynolds chuckled though deep inside, he was terrified. The realization that he might not make it back to fulfill his promise was crushing, yet he masked his fear with humor, a defense mechanism he had developed over years of service.

Graves turned to face Reynolds, placing a firm hand on his shoulder. "We're not done yet. As long as we're breathing, there's hope. We stick to the plan, fight smart, and we might just see another day."

Lin Feng descended slowly, his presence imposing and his gaze piercing. "Graves, always the optimist. It's a pity your optimism won't save you today."

Graves stared back, unflinching. "We didn't come this far to give up. You underestimate us."

The standoff was tense, with Lin Feng's associates closing in, encircling the team. The Blackwatch operatives and Navy Seals prepared for what seemed like their final stand, checking their weapons and exchanging determined glances.

Reynolds, feeling the weight of the moment, took a deep breath and thought of his girlfriend back in Hawaii. The promise of a future together, the plans they had made, and the life they hoped to build fueled his resolve. He whispered under his breath, "For her," before raising his weapon.

Graves nodded to his team, signaling them to be ready. "On my mark," he said, his voice steady.

Lin Feng laughed, a sound that sent shivers down their spines. "Bravery or foolishness? It does not. This ends now."

With an upward motion of his hand, huge biomass spikes erupted from the earth, the Blackwatch forces equipped with the Titan's Guard Mark II quickly moved to save the Navy Seals, getting them out of harm's way.

"It's futile," Lin Feng reiterated, his voice echoing ominously as the biomass tendrils, summoned from the dark and red magic circles, began their relentless assault.

The Blackwatch forces, quick on their feet, rallied around Graves' command.

"Don't let them catch you."

Graves weaved through the incoming tendrils of biomass. They were flexible, hard, and sharp as they sliced through the air.

Reynolds and his fellow Navy Seals provide covering fire, their bullets impacting Lin Feng's associates and keeping them at bay. They deployed smoke grenades and flashbangs to disrupt the enemy's vision and coordination.

As Graves neared, Lin Feng summoned more biomass tendrils to keep him at bay, but Graves with predictive features in his suit, was able to dodge it, and as he got closer, he fired a plasma cannon at point-blank, sending Lin Feng flying and crashing to the ground. Lin Feng let out a roar of pain and frustration as he hit the dirt.

"You bitch! How dare you push me down to the ground? Your insolence knows no bounds," Lin Feng spat, his voice seething with anger as he struggled to rise from the dirt.

Graves landed in front of him and cracked his knuckles. "I have been waiting for this moment. One-on-one, no one else around. I'm going to beat you to a pulp."

As Graves advanced on Lin Feng, determination burning in his eyes, Lin Feng's frustration reached a breaking point. His eyes began to glow ominously red, and with a furious burst of energy, he shot a powerful red laser beam from his eyes directly at Graves.

Reacting quickly, Graves activated the holographic shield of his advanced suit. The shield materialized in front of him just in time, absorbing the deadly red laser beam. The force of the attack pushed Graves backward, his feet slipping on the ground as he struggled to maintain his balance against the sheer power of Lin Feng's new attack.

The holographic shield crackled and shimmered under the intensity of the laser, but it held firm. Graves gritted his teeth and pushed back with all his strength, slowly but surely inching closer to Lin Feng.

Lin Feng's eyes continued to emit the red laser, his expression twisted in rage. He poured every ounce of his remaining energy into the attack, determined to eliminate Graves once and for all.

"So you won't stop it huh?" Graves huffed as he prepared his own beam, the plasma beam. The suit's chest started to glow with blue energy, and a beam of intense plasma energy surged forward from Graves' suit, countering Lin Feng's red laser. The two beams met in a powerful clash of energy, creating a dazzling display of light and sound.

The standoff between the two beams was intense, neither giving an inch. Graves' suit strained under the pressure, but he held his ground, determination, and willpower driving him forward. Lin Feng, despite his fury, was beginning to waver as Graves' plasma beam slowly pushed back his red laser.

With a final surge of energy, Graves' plasma beam overwhelmed Lin Feng's red laser, pushing it back and closing the distance between them.

Seeing his impending defeat, Lin Feng tried to redirect the energy into a last-ditch effort to escape, but it was too late. Graves unleashed the full force of his plasma beam, enveloping Lin Feng in a torrent of searing energy.

Lin Feng let out a guttural scream as the plasma beam consumed him.

"ARRRGGGGHHHH ENOUGH!"

Suddenly, dark energy exploded from Lin Feng's body as he underwent a terrifying transformation. His height increased, his body bulged with grotesque muscles, and his face contorted into a monstrous visage. One of his arms morphed into a razor-sharp blade, pulsating with an ominous energy.

Lin Feng's monstrous form roared in fury, and with a swift motion of his bladed arm, he slashed through the air, sending a wave of dark energy and cutting through the plasma beam. The beam dissipated, and Graves was thrown backward, crashing to the ground.

As the dust settled, Lin Feng stood tall, his devilish appearance radiating malevolence. His eyes, now glowing with an eerie red light, locked onto Graves with a sinister intensity. The left arm blade pulsed with a dark energy, ready for another attack.

"This is Specter-1. Lin Feng has transformed! I repeat, Lin Feng has transformed! All stations, keep those associates at bay, I'll handle this one."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 303 - 303: Graves vs Lin Feng Again
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"How foolish of you to believe that you can confront me alone," Lin Feng's voice echoed with a demonic edge, chilling the very air around him.

"Well…my friends are busy handling your harem so…yeah…I'm going to be the one who will beat you," Graves retorted, masking his fear with a touch of sarcasm. Deep inside, he knew the odds were stacked against him, but he had a duty to fulfill as a summoned defender. It would be an honor if he could put an end to this existential threat, even if it cost him his life.

However, as he stood there, facing the monstrous Lin Feng, memories of him with Emily flashed across his mind. Her beautiful smile, her warm voice, and the tender touch of her hand. These thoughts bolstered his resolve, giving him the strength to confront the impending battle, even against insurmountable odds.

He glanced over his shoulder, looking at his comrades who were fiercely fighting Lin Feng's associate. Seeing that they could handle themselves, he flickered his gaze back to Lin Feng.

"Let's start this," Graves muttered under his breath, his determination unwavering. Activating his plasma cannon, he fired a short burst at Lin Feng. The searing plasma streaked through the air, heading straight for Lin Feng's monstrous form.

With lightning reflexes, Lin Feng swung his bladed arm, intercepting the plasma projectiles.

The plasma beams clashed with his arm blade, causing a dazzling explosion of energy that lit up the battlefield.

Graves used the distraction to fly around in swift circles, creating distance between him and Lin Feng. He knew that getting too close to that monstrous form could be lethal, and he needed to assess his opponent's abilities carefully.

Lin Feng, now fully focused on Graves, let out a guttural growl as he swung his arm blade in an arc, sending a wave of dark energy slicing through the air, directly at Graves.

Graves veered to the side, narrowly avoiding the deadly attack. He could feel the dark energy grazing his suit, leaving scorched marks. This battle was going to push him to his limits, but he couldn't back down now.

Regaining his composure, Graves continued to fire plasma bursts at Lin Feng, trying to keep the demonic entity on the defensive as he searched for an opening. However—Lin Feng went on the offensive, the wings behind his back flapping powerfully as he closed the distance between him and Graves.

Graves knew he had to act fast, and with a quick thought command, his advanced suit materialized a plasma blade in his hand.

The plasma blade crackled to life, emitting a fierce blue glow. Lin Feng's monstrous form and Graves clashed in a flurry of blows, their weapons sparking and sizzling as they collided. Each strike sent shockwaves through the air, and the ground beneath them trembled from the force of their battle.

Graves fought with all his strength, his muscles straining as he parried Lin Feng's attacks. He could feel the heat radiating from the plasma blade as it clashed with Lin Feng's arm blade, creating blinding sparks.

Lin Feng's eyes glowed ominously, and his monstrous face contorted with rage as he pushed against Graves, his immense strength threatening to overwhelm him. Graves gritted his teeth, drawing on every ounce of his resolve and determination to hold his ground.

The two combatants were locked in a fierce struggle, neither willing to give an inch. Graves knew that this battle could go either way, and he couldn't afford to lose. With a surge of energy, he pushed back against Lin Feng, their plasma blades clashing once more, and the resulting explosion of sparks lit up the battlefield.

"I must admit…your suit is strong," Lin Feng commented. "But remove that…what are you?"

"A handsome special forces with a beautiful woman on his side that is definitely not mind-controlled to like me," Graves taunted, trying to provoke Lin Feng as he continued to parry the monstrous entity's attacks.

Lin Feng roared in fury, his monstrous form shaking with rage. He swung his bladed arm with even more ferocity, but Graves remained agile and managed to evade the deadly strikes.

"What's the matter? Can't handle a little banter while we fight?" Graves taunted,

Lin Feng's eyes burned with anger, and he lunged at Graves with renewed ferocity. His bladed arm swung in a deadly arc, but Graves deftly sidestepped the attack. He could feel the rush of wind as the blade sliced through the air inches from his body.

"So it was true huh? You mind-controlled those poor ladies to like you? How pathetic! You must be a loser in real life and decided everything has to end because of your twisted desires," Graves taunted, his words like a verbal jab aimed straight at Lin Feng's monstrous heart.

"SHUT UP!" Lin Feng's voice thundered, his rage reaching its peak. With a powerful downward slash, his bladed arm struck the ground, causing the earth to splinter and crack. The shockwave sent Graves tumbling backward, his advanced suit absorbing some of the impact to protect him from the devastating force.

Graves rolled to his feet, his plasma blade at the ready. He could see the rage in Lin Feng's eyes, the darkness that consumed him. The battle had taken a toll on both of them, but Graves knew that he couldn't afford to hold back now.

Lin Feng lunged at Graves once more, his bladed arm slashing through the air with terrifying speed. Graves parried the attack, but he could feel the immense strength behind each strike. Lin Feng was growing more dangerous with every passing moment.

As the two clashed, Graves noticed a pattern in Lin Feng's attacks. There was a split-second delay between each strike, a small window of opportunity. Graves decided to exploit it.

With lightning reflexes, Graves dodged to the side as Lin Feng's bladed arm slashed through the air. He countered with a swift strike of his own, his plasma blade cutting through Lin Feng's arm, causing dark energy to spill from the wound.

Lin Feng roared in pain, staggering back. Graves didn't let up, pressing his advantage. He launched a series of rapid strikes, aiming for the openings he had identified. Dark energy burst from Lin Feng's wounded arm as it was sliced further, and his monstrous form flickered.

Graves continued his assault, his determination unwavering. He knew that he had to finish this battle before Lin Feng could recover. And to end all of it, Graves plunged his plasma blade deep into Lin Feng's heart. Dark energy surged out from the wound, and Lin Feng's monstrous form convulsed in agony…for a moment.

"You think you have won?" Lin Feng laughed demonically.

Graves narrowly avoided the deadly strike from Lin Feng's arm blade by retracting his own plasma blade and leaping away just in time. The earth where he had stood a moment ago was cleaved apart as Lin Feng's blade struck with terrifying force.

"Okay…I think I can't kill this man alone, I'm going to need backup."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 304 - 304: It's Starting to Get Serious
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Specters…this is Specter-1, how are you doing up there?"

"We are busy boss—" responded one of the Blackwatch forces. Graves could hear through his comms the sound of intense gunfire and explosions echoing in the background.

"Boss, we've got our hands full with these associates," another voice chimed in, filled with the strain of combat.

"We're not holding up too well down here either," Graves muttered under his breath, his attention still locked on the looming threat of Lin Feng. "But we'll manage. Just keep those associates off our backs as long as you can."

"This is Specter-4, available for assist," came a new voice over the comms, providing a glimmer of hope.

"Specter-4, can you go to my current position? I'm going to need help on taking down Lin Feng."

"Solid copy, Specter-1, coming in hot," replied Specter-4.

As Graves continued to engage Lin Feng in battle, he activated the diagnostics interface on his Titan's Guard Mark II suit. The holographic display appeared before his eyes, showing an extensive list of data and status indicators.

Diagnostics Report - Titan's Guard Mark II

System Integrity: 67%

Power Distribution: Optimal

Armor Integrity:

Left Arm Plating: 76%

Right Arm Plating: 68%

Chest Plate: 82%

Leg Armor: 93%

Helmet Integrity: 95%

Weapon Systems:

Plasma Cannon: Online

Plasma Blade: Online

Targeting Systems: Optimal

Ammo Reserves: 42%

Auxiliary Systems:

Life Support: Functional

Communications Array: Optimal

Mobility Systems: Optimal

Energy Core: Stable

Error Logs:

Minor damage detected in the right arm plating. Recommend immediate repair post-battle.

Energy core fluctuations detected, but within acceptable parameters.

Communication array received minor interference, affecting long-range transmission.

Graves couldn't afford to be distracted by the damage, but he knew that some repairs would be necessary once they dealt with Lin Feng and his associates. The Titan's Guard Mark II had served him well in this battle, but it was showing signs of wear and tear.

"What's the matter? Are you going to stand there?" Lin Feng taunted.

Graves extended his arms forward and fired a plasma cannon that was simply swatted away by Lin Feng's bladed arm.

"Have no more tricks up your sleeves?" Lin Feng taunted as he began to menacingly walk forward. He was not rushing anymore, as if believing that he'll win this fight.

Just then, Specter-4 swooped in from above, firing a volley of miniature missiles from the suit's shoulder-mounted launchers. The projectiles homed in on Lin Feng, exploding upon impact and creating a series of fiery explosions around him. Dark energy emanated from Lin Feng's body as he grunted in pain from the concussive blasts.

Graves watched as Specter-4 landed next to him. "Right on time. How's your suit?"

Specter-4 quickly ran a systems check. "It's still optimal, sir. It can take a beating from Lin Feng. You... looked rough, sir," Specter-4 remarked with concern.

Graves nodded, acknowledging the damage to his own Titan's Guard Mark II suit. "We'll have to make do. Lin Feng won't go down without a fight."

Lin Feng, recovering from the missile barrage, seemed to regain his composure. His monstrous form radiated dark energy as he grinned menacingly at the two defenders.

Without wasting a moment, Lin Feng lunged at Graves and Specter-4, his bladed arm slashing through the air. Graves and Specter-4 split apart, narrowly avoiding the deadly strike. The force of the attack created a shockwave that sent debris flying in all directions.

Graves and Specter-4 fired their plasma cannons simultaneously from two different directions, creating a crossfire that forced Lin Feng to defend himself from multiple angles. The searing plasma beams struck Lin Feng's monstrous form, causing him to roar in pain.

Despite the relentless assault, Lin Feng continued to press forward, his bladed arm deflecting some of the plasma blasts while others seared into his flesh. He showed no signs of slowing down, his determination unwavering.

"This is getting pointless," Graves gritted his teeth. If only there was a way for them to know if their attack was damaging him or not, but there was none.

Lin Feng jumped forward, his eyes locked on Specter-4. With lightning speed, he managed to catch Specter-4 within his grasp, his monstrous hand closing around the Blackwatch force's suit. Specter-4 struggled to break free, but Lin Feng's grip was like a vice.

Desperation in his voice, Specter-4 yelled through the comms, "Specter-1, I'm in trouble here!"

Graves could see the dire situation his comrade was in. He needed to act quickly. He aimed his plasma cannon at Lin Feng, but before he could fire, Lin Feng's monstrous arm shifted, placing Specter-4 between them, effectively using the Blackwatch force as a shield.

Specter-4 continued to struggle within Lin Feng's grasp, his plasma blade extended and attempting to cut through the monstrous arm that held him. But it proved futile as the flesh seemed to reinforce itself upon contact with the blade, preventing any significant damage.

Graves knew he had to find a way to break Lin Feng's hold on Specter-4. He quickly assessed the situation, trying to spot any weaknesses or vulnerabilities in Lin Feng's monstrous form.

"Argh…" Lin Feng began to tighten his grip on Specter-4 causing him to gasp for air.

Graves couldn't let his comrade be crushed by the relentless grip of Lin Feng. He fired another plasma shot at Lin Feng, aiming for the same damaged area on his chest plate.

But at this point, Lin Feng is not minding the damage to his chest plate. The searing plasma shot struck Lin Feng, causing him to grunt in pain, but his focus remained on crushing Specter-4 within his grasp.

Specter-4's struggles grew weaker as the monstrous grip tightened further. He gasped for air, his vision starting to blur. Graves knew that time was running out. Desperation fueled his actions.

Graves swooped in, determined to cut through Lin Feng's monstrous arm and free Specter-4. He activated his suit's plasma blade, the searing energy blade humming to life. With all his strength, he brought the blade down on Lin Feng's arm.

The plasma blade made contact with Lin Feng's flesh, but to Graves' shock, it barely made a scratch. Lin Feng's blood, solidified on contact with the plasma, acted as a near-impenetrable armor. The blade couldn't cut through.

Specter-4's struggles grew weaker as his suit's systems began to fail. His last words came through the comms in a strained whisper, "Specter-1... help…"

Graves aimed the cannon at Lin Feng's head, making one last ditch effort but Lin Feng's eyes glowed brightly red, and a beam similar to earlier was shot out from his eyes, pushing Graves backward with a powerful force. The searing energy beam narrowly missed him but left his suit's systems heavily damaged. Graves struggled to regain control as alarms blared in his helmet.

Lin Feng, still holding Specter-4 in his grasp, lifted his monstrous arm, preparing to strike the final blow. Graves reached out his hands to his comrade, but it was too late, Lin Feng pierced Specter-4's chest with his bladed arm, ending his life. Specter-4's suit went limp, and his lifeless body hung in Lin Feng's grasp.

"Hmm…this is odd, this is not the usual reaction when you see your comrade die before your eyes. Well…it must be a summon," Lin Feng commented, as he threw Specter-4's corpse to the ground. "Let's see. I need to inflict emotional damage on you or on your boss. Let me ask you this…Graves…did your boss consider you as a friend not a summon?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 305 - 305: Revealing the Final Plan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the command center of Blackwatch, screens displayed the ongoing operation. One showed a first-person view of the joint task forces as they battled Lin Feng and his associates. Another displayed a live feed from inside the Cheyenne Mountain Complex, revealing President William and his staff.

President William looked bewildered by the intense battle playing out before him. He had always thought such scenes belonged in movies or fiction, not in reality.

"So that is Lin Feng huh?" Barry said.

"Yes, Mr. President, that's him in the flesh and blood. But our men won't last there for long. This mission was supposed to confirm whether Lin Feng is alive or not, but based on what we're seeing, we have our confirmation. Our men have a high chance of not making it out of there alive."

As Richard spoke, a somber atmosphere settled in the command center. The reality of the situation weighed heavily on everyone present.

"Then we should extract our men out there," President Barry suggested.
