"Keep the sonar active and maintain constant vigilance. Lin Feng's capabilities are not to be underestimated. He could deploy threats at any moment."

The operator nodded, turning back to his instruments, the rhythmic beep of the sonar echoing softly in the background. Richard then shifted his attention back to the broader strategy at play.

"Keep me updated on any changes. I want hourly reports on our fleet's status and immediate notification if anything unusual is detected," Richard commanded. And before he could hear the acknowledgment from his crew, something happened.

Suddenly, the calm in the command center was shattered by an urgent voice from one of the sonar operators. "Sir, we've detected something massive moving beneath the water, directly in the path of our fleet. The sonar signature is... it's huge, resembling that of the Abyssal Leviathan we've briefed on."

Richard's focus snapped to the operator, his previous instructions momentarily forgotten. "Show me," he demanded, his tone laced with urgency.

The operator quickly adjusted the display, bringing up a live feed of the sonar readings on the main screen for all to see. A massive, shadowy outline moved beneath the waves, its size and movement patterns unmistakably predatory.

Richard's gaze hardened as he assessed the threat. "Inform the fleet immediately. They need to be on high alert. Deploy anti-submarine warfare units and prepare for engagement. We can't let it reach the fleet."

"Sir, yes, sir!" the operator responded, fingers flying over the controls to relay the critical information to the fleet.

***

Just beneath the fleet, the Virginia Class Submarine of the Blackwatch Naval Forces was already in a state of heightened alertness. Captain Jameson, commanding the vessel, received the immediate directive from Blackwatch Command. His response was swift and decisive. "Battle stations," he ordered, his voice echoing through the submarine's intercom system.

The crew, well-trained and highly experienced, sprang into action, each member knowing their role with precision.

The Virginia Class, one of the most advanced submarines in the Blackwatch fleet, was equipped for a variety of missions, including anti-submarine warfare (ASW), intelligence gathering, and direct engagement with underwater threats. The captain's priority was to arm the submarine's weapons systems for engagement.

"Load all torpedo tubes, prepare to launch Mark 48 ADCAP torpedoes on my command," Captain Jameson instructed the weapons officer. The Mark 48 ADCAP (Advanced Capability) torpedoes were designed for precision strikes against high-value underwater targets, capable of destroying enemy submarines and large aquatic threats like the Abyssal Leviathan with their high-explosive warheads.

Simultaneously, the sonar team intensified their monitoring, tracking the massive entity moving toward the fleet. "Update on target's bearing and speed," Captain Jameson requested, his eyes fixed on the digital displays showing the leviathan's position.

The sonar technician, analyzing the incoming data, responded, "Target is maintaining course, speed consistent with previous sightings of Abyssal Leviathans. Estimated range to target, 2000 yards and closing."

"Very well, bring us to attack depth, prepare for silent running," the captain ordered, his mind focused on outmaneuvering the creature and positioning his submarine for a direct hit. The Virginia Class Submarine's stealth capabilities were unmatched, allowing it to approach targets undetected under the right conditions.

As the submarine adjusted its depth, Captain Jameson reviewed the engagement plan with his crew. "We have one shot at this. The Mark 48s are our best bet. Once in position, we'll fire a spread to ensure a hit. Keep an eye on the sonar; I don't want this thing to surprise us."

Minutes later, the submarine was in position. "Target is within range. Fire," Captain Jameson commanded, his voice calm but authoritative.

The submarine shuddered slightly as the torpedo tubes expelled their deadly payload. The Mark 48 ADCAP torpedoes, propelled by their own engines, raced through the water towards the Abyssal Leviathan, guided by the sonar data fed into their systems.

"Torpedoes away, tracking towards the target," announced the weapons officer. The crew waited in tense silence as the torpedoes closed the distance to the leviathan.

With the first salvo of Mark 48 ADCAP torpedoes launched, Captain Jameson and his crew aboard the Virginia Class Submarine awaited the outcome of their attack on the detected massive underwater threat. The Mark 48 ADCAP, known for its advanced guidance and explosive power, was their best weapon against such a formidable adversary.

"Torpedo impact in 3... 2... 1..." the weapons officer counted down, tension palpable in the air.

The expected confirmation of a successful hit came through. "Direct hits confirmed, sir," reported the sonar technician. "Analyzing damage assessment now."

However, the anticipated outcome was not as straightforward as they had hoped. The sonar feedback showed disturbances in the water, indicative of the target's continued movement. "Sir, the target... it's still advancing. The Abyssal Leviathan appears to have sustained the torpedo hits but remains operational," the sonar technician updated, disbelief evident in his voice.

Captain Jameson's expression turned grim. As expected, this wouldn't be easy.

"Reload torpedo tubes. Prepare for a second salvo. This time, aim for any identified weak points or vital areas," he ordered, not willing to concede defeat. "Adjust the torpedo's targeting parameters based on the new sonar data. We need to maximize the impact."

The submarine's crew worked with renewed urgency, loading the torpedo tubes for another attack. The Mark 48 ADCAP torpedoes were not only capable of high-speed pursuit but could also be re-targeted mid-course based on updated sonar information, a feature Captain Jameson intended to utilize to its fullest.

"Torpedo tubes loaded, sir. Awaiting your command," announced the weapons officer, ready for the second strike.

Captain Jameson took a moment to assess the situation, considering the leviathan's trajectory and the fleet's safety. "Fire when ready. Let's make sure it counts this time," he commanded.

As the second salvo of torpedoes raced through the depths towards the leviathan, the crew held their breath, hoping for a decisive outcome. The anticipation was cut short by the sonar's confirmation of another series of impacts.

"Sir, multiple hits confirmed on the Leviathan's midsection. Observing significant behavioral changes in the target," the sonar technician reported a hint of optimism in his voice.

However, the Abyssal Leviathan's resilience was beyond their expectations. While the torpedoes inflicted considerable damage, the creature continued its advance, though at a reduced speed.

"Damage inflicted, but the target remains a threat. Sir, it's slowing down but not out," the sonar technician updated.

"Shit….looks like we are going to need backup from the fleet. Contact the Arleigh-Burke and the Ticonderoga Class."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 317 - 317: Missile Porn
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Following the Virginia Class Submarine's engagement with the Abyssal Leviathan, Captain Jameson realized the magnitude of the threat demanded more firepower than his submarine alone could provide.

Recognizing the need for immediate reinforcements, he initiated a distress call to the surface fleet, specifically targeting the Arleigh Burke-class destroyers and Ticonderoga-class cruisers for their advanced anti-submarine warfare (ASW) capabilities.

The Arleigh Burke-class destroyers, equipped with the AN/SQQ-89(V)15 sonar systems, were among the first to respond. These systems, designed for the detection and tracking of submarine threats, could also be repurposed to engage large underwater entities like the Leviathan.

The destroyers' commanders, upon receiving the submarine's report, ordered their crews to action stations, preparing for an unconventional engagement.

"Load the torpedo tubes with Mark 54 lightweight torpedoes," commanded the captain of the nearest Arleigh Burke-class destroyer. The Mark 54, primarily designed for shallow-water anti-submarine engagements, was chosen for its agility and explosive power, deemed suitable for the leviathan's predicted depth and size.

Simultaneously, the Ticonderoga-class cruisers, known for their command and control capabilities, began coordinating the attack. Their AEGIS Combat System, integrated with the SPY-1 radar, provided a comprehensive battlefield overview, allowing for precise targeting information to be shared across the fleet.

"Deploy the RUM-139 VL-ASROC," ordered the Ticonderoga's captain. The Vertical Launch Anti-Submarine Rocket, equipped with a Mark 54 torpedo as its payload, could be launched from the cruiser's vertical launch system (VLS), offering a long-range engagement option against the underwater threat.

As the ASW units readied their weapons, the fleet's helicopter detachment, MH-60R Seahawks, equipped with dipping sonar and air-launched torpedoes, took to the skies. These helicopters would serve as the eyes and ears of the fleet, extending the ASW engagement range and providing real-time data back to the ships and the Virginia Class submarine.

Within minutes, the combined ASW arsenal was ready for deployment. The Arleigh Burke-class destroyers launched their Mark 54 torpedoes, aiming for the coordinates provided by the Virginia Class's latest sonar readings.

Almost simultaneously, the Ticonderoga-class cruisers fired their ASROC missiles, the rockets arching gracefully before plunging into the sea, releasing their torpedo payloads closer to the leviathan's location.

The MH-60R Seahawks, hovering at a safe distance, dropped sonobuoys to monitor the leviathan's movements and confirm the hits from the deployed torpedoes. Their pilots maintained constant communication with the surface fleet, relaying updates and adjusting tactics as the situation evolved.

As the torpedoes and ASROC payloads approached their target, the anticipation among the fleet was palpable. Each Mark 54 torpedo, upon reaching its programmed search depth, activated its onboard sonar, homing in on the massive signature of the Abyssal Leviathan.

The explosions that followed were monitored closely, both visually and through acoustic sensors, to assess the effectiveness of the strikes.

Admiral Johnson of the Blackwatch Naval Fleet demanded an assessment from his team as the last of the torpedoes and ASROC payloads delivered their explosive charges against the Abyssal Leviathan.

"Sir, the initial damage assessment is in," reported a tactical officer, scanning the incoming data from the sonobuoys and the MH-60R Seahawks overhead. "The Leviathan has sustained multiple hits from our Mark 54 torpedoes and ASROC payloads. We're observing a decrease in its velocity and some erratic movement patterns. However, it's still active."

"I have reviewed the details of the Abyssal Leviathan when it was encountered in the Aparri. As long as we hit it multiple times, it will have no chance but to emerge, and when it does, we will unleash hell on it. So continue firing until it surfaces," Admiral Johnson instructed firmly.

The fleet continued their assault, coordinating their efforts to maximize the impact on the leviathan lurking beneath the waves. The Arleigh Burke-class destroyers, now in a synchronized attack pattern with the Ticonderoga-class cruisers, launched another volley of Mark 54 torpedoes and ASROC missiles, each weapon system programmed to target the leviathan's last known position.

"Mark 54s away, ASROC in flight," confirmed the weapons coordinator aboard one of the Ticonderoga-class cruisers, his eyes fixed on the tracking screens. The sea's surface was again disturbed by the impact of the weapons, sending columns of water into the air.

The MH-60R Seahawks continued their overwatch, circling the area and providing real-time feedback to the ships below.

Sonobuoys are detecting significant distress from the target. It's moving erratically, possibly preparing to surface," relayed one of the Seahawk pilots through the fleet's communication network.

Admiral Johnson, receiving the pilot's report, prepared for the next phase of the engagement.

"All units, prepare for surface engagement. Ready the 5-inch guns and CIWS. The moment it breaches, we hit it with everything we've got," he ordered, anticipating the leviathan's emergence.

The crew members aboard the fleet readied their weapons, tension mounting as they awaited the appearance of the leviathan.

Minutes passed, each second stretching longer than the last, until finally, a massive form broke the surface of the water. The Abyssal Leviathan, wounded and enraged, emerged, presenting a target unlike any the fleet had faced before.

"Now!" Admiral Johnson shouted, and the fleet responded. The 5-inch guns of the destroyers and cruisers roared to life, sending a barrage of high-explosive shells toward the monstrous form before them. The CIWS systems, typically used for anti-air defense, were repurposed to engage the leviathan, their rapid-fire capabilities adding to the onslaught.

Not only that, but surface-to-air missiles were also employed, like the RIM-162 Evolved SeaSparrow Missile (ESSM), adapted for a surface target in this unique scenario, to strike at the more exposed and presumably vulnerable sections of the Leviathan as it surfaced.

The ESSM, originally designed for air defense, was now being used in an improvised role to exploit any weakness in the Leviathan's defenses, demonstrating the fleet's flexibility and adaptability in combat situations.

In addition to the repurposed air defense missiles, the fleet decided to utilize land-attack missiles to increase the pressure on the Abyssal Leviathan, now vulnerable on the surface. The choice fell on the Tomahawk Land Attack Missiles (TLAMs) from the Ticonderoga-class cruisers' vertical launch systems.

"Prepare to fire TLAMs, target the Leviathan's exposed areas," ordered Admiral Johnson, recognizing the opportunity to use the Tomahawks' long-range and high-explosive capabilities to their advantage. The Ticonderoga's weapons team quickly programmed the missiles' guidance systems with the leviathan's coordinates, ensuring a direct hit.

As the Tomahawk missiles were launched, their flight paths were adjusted to descend upon the leviathan from above, exploiting its massive size and the lack of maneuverability on the surface.

The impact of the Tomahawk missiles was devastating. Each explosion upon the leviathan's back caused massive wounds, further weakening the creature and causing it to thrash violently in the water. The combined assault of high-explosive shells, CIWS fire, and now Tomahawk missiles overwhelmed the leviathan's natural defenses, inflicting critical damage.

"Direct hits confirmed, observing significant damage to the leviathan," reported the tactical officer, monitoring the results of the Tomahawk strikes. "Its effectiveness is significantly reduced, sir."

Admiral Johnson, satisfied with the outcome, gave the order to continue the barrage.

"All units, maintain fire. Let's finish this," he commanded. The relentless attack continued, with every available weapon system directed at the wounded leviathan.

As the final rounds of ammunition and missiles were expended, the leviathan's movements finally ceased. It floated lifelessly on the surface.

"Cease fire," Admiral Johnson ordered, as it became clear that the leviathan was no longer a threat. "All units, stand down."

***

In the Blackwatch command center, Richard confirmed Admiral Johnson through his system. There a notification was displayed of him eliminating the Abyssal Leviathan.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 318 - 318: Progressing Smoothly
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"What kind of monster was that?!" President Barry exclaimed through his monitor. He just watched the Blackwatch Naval Fleet rain hellfire on the aquatic monster, and it was his first time seeing that kind of monster. Throughout his career, having been introduced to a lot of confidential information that would definitely scare the public, that monster was one of them.

"It's one of Lin Feng's minions," Richard answered simply. "We called them, Abyssal Leviathan. Well, they are quite strong, like the myth of the Kraken. Can pull down a warship and even crush them using their tentacles. However, they are vulnerable to conventional weapons so as long as we keep pummeling them with leads and arrows, they'll... eventually succumb," Richard continued.

President Barry, absorbing the explanation, nodded thoughtfully. "How many monsters we haven't seen except from the video footage you have sent us?"

Richard paused for a moment, considering how much to disclose. "Mr. President, the Abyssal Leviathans are among the more formidable creatures we've encountered, but they're not the only ones. Lin Feng has managed to amass a variety of creatures, each with unique abilities and threats. However, our encounters have been limited, and we're still gathering intelligence on the full extent of his arsenal."

"The exact number is uncertain. We've seen evidence of other entities, some terrestrial, some aerial, beyond the Leviathans. Our teams are working around the clock to identify, classify, and develop countermeasures for each threat," Richard elaborated, his tone serious.

President Barry leaned back, processing the information. "On behalf of the United States, I would like to apologize for lacking information on the zombie apocalypse. We were preserving what was left and had been hiding deep in the mountains while we came up with plans to reclaim our planet. We don't know if it'll come to this degree."

"Mr. President, these are unprecedented times. Every nation has been forced to make difficult choices to ensure the survival of its people. What matters now is that we're united in our efforts to reclaim our planet and protect humanity from these threats."

He continued, "Blackwatch has been on the frontline, encountering these threats head-on. With the support of the United States, we're making significant strides. Speaking of which, forces are nearing Hong Kong. It's time that we execute phase 2 of our plan."

The Phase 2 Richard is telling was Operation Clear Sky. Its objective is to clear Hong Kong of hostile forces, and by hostile, they meant zombies.

The first part would be through the use of Air Forces, fighter jets, bomber aircraft, gunships, and close-in support aircraft to conduct preliminary strikes on key enemy positions, aiming to soften up defenses and eliminate high-value targets that could pose a significant threat to the ground forces.

"We'll be using precision airstrikes to minimize collateral damage while maximizing the impact on enemy forces. Drones will provide real-time surveillance, ensuring that our strikes are as effective as possible," Richard outlined the air strategy. Following the aerial bombardment, our ground troops will move in. And clear everything that's left."

"Okay, let's execute that phase," Barry said with a determined tone.

Hearing that, Richard flickered his gaze to Mark. "Let's start warning the citizens of Hong Kong, shall we?"

Mark nodded and began relaying the command to the EC-130H Compass Call flying over Hong Kong Airspace.

***

The EC-130H Compass Call, a specialized aircraft equipped with advanced electronic warfare systems, was already airborne, circling at high altitudes over Hong Kong. Its crew, experts in electronic warfare and communication interception, had been briefed on their mission: to hack into the public address systems scattered across the city and broadcast a critical warning to the citizens.

The goal was to minimize civilian casualties during the upcoming air strikes against the zombie hordes.

Inside the aircraft, the crew worked diligently to establish connections with the city's communication infrastructure. The operator responsible for the hack carefully bypassed security protocols, gaining access to the public address systems without triggering any alarms.

Once the system was compromised, the technician nodded to the mission commander, indicating readiness to broadcast. The commander, after a final check with the team, gave the nod to proceed.

"Attention, citizens of Hong Kong. This is Blackwatch Private Military Company, in cooperation with the United States Government. We urge you to seek immediate shelter and brace for impact. Air forces from Blackwatch and the United States are about to commence strategic bombing operations against concentrated zones of hostile forces within the city.

For your safety, remain indoors and do not attempt to leave your location until rescue and search teams have reached you. Help is on the way. We are here to assist and ensure your safety during these operations. Thank you for your cooperation."

The broadcast was repeated multiple times, alternating between English and Chinese to ensure comprehension across Hong Kong's diverse population. The EC-130H Compass Call crew monitored the transmission's reach, adjusting frequencies and power outputs to penetrate as deep into the urban fabric as possible, ensuring no corner of the city remained uninformed.

Simultaneously, the operation's air component was in full swing. Fighter jets such as the F-35s, equipped with precision-guided munitions, began their sorties, targeting identified concentrations of undead with surgical strikes designed to inflict maximum damage on the enemy while preserving the city's infrastructure as much as possible.

Bomber aircraft, such as the B-52 flying at higher altitudes, released their payloads over larger infested areas, each bomb was carefully chosen for its yield and blast radius to ensure the eradication of the undead with minimal risk to any surviving civilians.

Gunships, specifically the AC-130, flying lower and slower, provided close air support, their cannons, and rockets shredding through the hordes. These aerial platforms were coordinated through a network of drones that hovered above, relaying real-time battlefield data back to command centers where analysts and officers assessed the situation, directing strikes where they were needed most.

On the ground, Citizens, already wary from months of living under the shadow of Lin Feng's reign of terror, took the warning seriously. Homes and shelters across Hong Kong braced themselves, windows shuttered, and doors locked as families huddled together, waiting for the storm to pass.

They hoped that the explosions would kill all the remaining zombies.

The strikes were just the beginning of Operation Clear Sky. Following the air campaign, Blackwatch and U.S. ground forces would move in, sweeping through the city in a coordinated effort to eliminate any remaining threats. Armored vehicles, including M1 Abrams tanks and Stryker armored fighting vehicles, would lead the charge, their heavy firepower clearing a path for infantry units to follow.

These ground forces were equipped with the latest in personal protective gear and armed with an arsenal designed to take on both the undead and any of Lin Feng's more exotic creations. Special units, trained in urban warfare and counter-zombie tactics cleared buildings, sewers, and subways, ensuring no corner of Hong Kong remained a haven for the enemy.

Richard watched the map of Hong Kong, which was layered in red, slowly getting crept by a green layer, signifying the progression of Operation Clear Sky and the reclaiming of Hong Kong from the undead and Lin Feng's control.

"Things are going as planned," Richard muttered under his breath.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 319 - 319: The Fight in Hong Kong
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Hoh—This is interesting,"

Three of Lin Feng's associates stood over the hill, overlooking Hong Kong. Warmachines such as tanks and infantry fighting vehicles, and aircraft such as attack helicopters, fighter jets, gunships, and close-in support aircraft were all visible in the distance, engaging in a fierce battle to reclaim the city from the clutches of Lin Feng's undead army.

The air was thick with the sound of gunfire, explosions, and the distant roar of engines.

Among the trio was Zayra, a figure shrouded in an aura of malevolence, her eyes glowing with a dark, unnatural light. She was known for her ability to manipulate corrupted plants, turning the earth's flora into deadly weapons at her whim. Standing beside her were two other formidable associates of Lin Feng.

"Zayra, do not underestimate them. They have defeated our master's powerful summons. We don't want to repeat the mistake Seo-Jun, Crimson, and Violet did when confronting the Blackwatch. Let's not be the addition to Lin Feng's list of failures," Vega cautioned coldly.

She stood cloaked in shadows, the ends of it writhing as it touched the ground, ready to be wielded as a weapon against their foes.

Maiden was already surveying the battlefield for the most strategic points of engagement. "We should split up and kill all Blackwatch and the United States Forces subtly and covertly. We don't want to attract the attention of their huge caliber weapons. I'll take North."

"I'll take the East," Vega said.

"And I'll take the West," Zayra said.

With that, they split up, each heading in their designated direction with a specific plan to disrupt and dismantle the opposition's efforts from within.

***

Somewhere within Hong Kong, an Infantry Fighting Vehicle, the M1128 Mobile Gun System was roaming down the streets along with ten infantry units. They cleared nearby storefronts and alleys, vigilant for any signs of the undead or Lin Feng's minions. Their mission was to secure and hold a strategic junction that would allow for better movement of troops and resources throughout the city.

As the M1128 Mobile Gun System maneuvered through the ruined streets, its crew remained alert, the vehicle's powerful 105mm cannon ready to engage any threat. The infantry units, equipped with standard issue rifles and supported by a couple of squad automatic weapons, moved in a tight formation, covering each other's flanks.

Suddenly, the silence was broken by the rustling sound of movement from a nearby alley. The soldiers immediately tightened their formation, weapons aimed toward the source of the noise. Out of the shadows emerged a horde of zombies.

Without hesitation, the gunner in the M1128 opened fire, the cannon's explosive rounds decimating the front ranks of the horde, while the infantry engaged with rifle fire, cutting down the undead before they could get close.

"Clear!" shouted one of the infantries.

"Let's keep moving."

The squad resumed their movement, heading to whatever led them. Moments later, one of the infantry noticed something.

"Do you see that?" he said.

"What?" His comrade asked.

"There, it looks like there is a woman over there," the soldier pointed.

"Help! I'm alone and I'm scared," said the woman in English, running up to them.

"Stop! Stay where you are!" said the lead soldier, prompting those behind him to aim their rifles at the girl.

"I'm not your enemy! I'm not an enemy!" shouted the woman, her voice trembling with fear as she continued to approach, her hands raised in what seemed like a gesture of surrender.

But just as the soldiers began to lower their guard, the woman's expression twisted into a malevolent smirk, and with a swift motion, she raised her hands high into the air. Suddenly, from the ground beneath her feet, corrupted vines shot forth with alarming speed, snaking toward the soldiers with deadly intent.

Realizing the imminent danger, the soldiers reacted swiftly. The lead soldier shouted a warning, and the squad immediately opened fire, unleashing a hail of bullets toward the woman. The gunfire tore through her body with brutal force, causing her to stumble backward before collapsing to the ground in a heap.

However, their momentary relief was short-lived as the woman's body began to disintegrate, revealing a mass of twisted, corrupted vines in her place. Before the soldiers could comprehend what was happening, more sharp vines erupted from all directions, piercing through their armor and flesh.

Panic ensued as the soldiers fought desperately to defend themselves, but it was futile against the relentless onslaught of the corrupted vines. One by one, they fell, their screams echoing through the desolate streets. However—inside the M1128 Mobile Gun System, the radio officer called for help.

"This is M1128 Mobile Gun System reporting from sector 7G in Hong Kong! We are under attack by one of Lin Feng's associates! We've encountered a hostile entity capable of manipulating corrupted vines. Our squad is being overwhelmed, requesting immediate backup and air support! I repeat we need backup and air support!"

"This is Blackwatch, we've received your distress call. Hang tight, reinforcements are on the way. Hold your position and continue to engage the enemy. Air support is en route to provide assistance. Stay strong, help is on the way."

As the radio transmission crackled to life inside the M1128 Mobile Gun System, the crew's hopes for assistance surged. However, their relief was short-lived as the situation escalated outside.

Zayra had stealthily closed in on the infantry unit. With a flick of her hand, she unleashed a wave of corrupted vines that entangled and ensnared the soldiers, rendering them helpless. Then, moments later, their life was cut short. Leaving only her and the M1128.

The crew of the M1128 knew it was up to them until help arrived.

"Okay, that's some sneaky bastard. Let's show her the power of the M1128 shall we boys?"

"Yes sir!"

The M1128 7.62 mm machine gun; and M240C coaxial machine gun began sputtering bullets towards Zayra.

Zayra seemed confident that she could block those calibers, so she raised a wall of vines, forming a protective barrier in front of her. However it didn't go as planned, the 7.62mm was enough to pierce through that wall of vines like a cardboard box.

As the bullets tore through her defenses, Zayra staggered backward, her expression contorted in pain and anger. Before she could react, the main armament of the M1128 unleashed its devastating firepower.

With a deafening roar, the cannon fired, sending a high-velocity shell hurtling towards Zayra. The explosion upon impact sent shockwaves rippling through the air, engulfing Zayra in a fiery blast. The force of the explosion lifted her off her feet, hurling her through the air before she crashed to the ground with a bone-jarring thud.

"Get that you son of a bitch!" the commander cursed.

Chapter 320 - 320: Zayra's Powers

Blackwatch Command Center.

"What was the report again? Lin Feng's associate?" President Barry asked Richard.

Richard, pacing back and forth along the command center floor, answered. "It's those people that Lin Feng corrupted, and have them under his control through magic. Though technically we don't have a term for magic yet. But that's basically the idea. Those associates are as powerful as his strongest summons, they can wield magic like straight out of fantasy."

President Barry nodded, his expression grave. "We can't afford to underestimate them. Have we dispatched any teams to deal with this threat?"

Richard nodded. "Yes, sir. We've sent multiple squads to assist the M1128 Mobile Gun System that reported the encounter. Additionally, air support is en route to provide backup and assistance."

President Barry glanced at the large monitor displaying real-time battlefield data. "How's the situation on the ground?"

Richard checked the latest updates on his tablet. "It's still a fierce battle, sir. Our forces are engaged in heavy combat with Lin Feng's minions, and casualties are mounting. But with the reinforcements we've dispatched, we're hoping to turn the tide."

President Barry clenched his jaw, his eyes reflecting determination. "Keep me updated on any developments. We need to coordinate our efforts and ensure that we crush Lin Feng's forces before they can cause any further damage."

With a nod, Richard continued to monitor the situation. "All stations, this is Eagle Actual. Continue what you are all doing, I want all those zombies eliminated within 48 hours."

In the sector where the M1128 faced Zayra.

Zayra staggered as she rose to her feet, her eyes locked on the tank that sent her flying.

She puffed. She did expect that those heavy machines could take her on, but not to a degree that she would be defeated easily by a single mobile unit.

"It can't be helped, I guess I have to do this," Zayra muttered under her breath, and extended her hand forward. The ground shook as a twisted thicket sprouted beneath the M1128.

The vines snaked around the M1128's tracks, attempting to immobilize the vehicle. Inside the M1128, the crew immediately recognized the threat and sprang into action.

"Sir, we've got vines wrapping around the tracks! We're losing mobility!" shouted one of the crew members.

The commander of the M1128 wasted no time. "Engage the tracks! Try to break free!"

The driver maneuvered the vehicle as best as they could, trying to shake off the entangling vines. Meanwhile, the gunner adjusted their aim, targeting the source of the vines with the M1128's main cannon.

With a thunderous boom, the cannon fired, sending a high-velocity round towards Zayra's location. The explosive impact blasted apart the thicket of vines, momentarily halting Zayra's assault.

However, Zayra was quick to retaliate. With a wave of her hand, she conjured a barrier of dense foliage, deflecting the incoming projectile.

"Keep firing! Don't let up!" ordered the M1128 commander.

The crew continued to unleash a barrage of gunfire and cannon rounds, relentlessly pounding Zayra's defenses. However, this time, Zayra moved from one place to another, taking advantage of the slow swivel rate of the M1128 to evade the incoming fire.

Inside the M1128, the crew grappled with the challenge of tracking Zayra's elusive movements.

"She's too fast! I can't get a clear shot!" the gunner shouted in frustration.

The commander gritted their teeth, eyes glued to the monitor displaying Zayra's movements. "Keep trying! We can't let her get the upper hand!"

Despite their efforts, Zayra continued to evade their attacks, weaving through the debris-strewn streets with uncanny agility. The crew of the M1128 knew they were locked in a deadly game of cat and mouse, with Zayra holding the advantage in maneuverability.

With a determined grimace, Zayra extended her arms, channeling her dark magic through the twisted vines snaking around the M1128. With a surge of power, the vines tightened their grip, lifting the heavy vehicle off the ground with surprising ease.

Inside the M1128, the crew braced themselves as the vehicle was hoisted into the air, their training kicking in as they prepared for the inevitable impact.

"Brace for impact! Hold on tight!" shouted the commander, gripping the controls tightly.

The crew clung to their stations as the M1128 was violently thrashed about by the force of Zayra's attack. Metal groaned and strained under the pressure as the vehicle was slammed back down onto the cracked asphalt with a resounding crash.

The impact reverberated through the interior of the M1128, sending shockwaves rippling through the crew. Despite the chaos, they quickly regained their composure, assessing the damage and determining their next course of action.

"Damage report! Status of the systems!" barked the commander, their voice cutting through the chaos.

"Tracks are damaged, but operational! The main cannon is still online, but we've lost targeting capability!" reported the gunner.

The driver swiftly assessed the situation, their hands flying over the controls as they fought to regain control of the vehicle. "We're mobile, but it's gonna be rough!"

!

As the crew scrambled to recover from the brutal assault, Zayra wasted no time in launching her next attack. With a sinister grin, she summoned another wave of thicket vines, this time with a deadly purpose.

The twisted vines slithered with malevolent intent, piercing through the underside of the M1128 with frightening precision. The crew inside the vehicle could only watch in horror as the sharp tendrils tore through the armor plating, breaching the interior of the tank with alarming ease.

Panic spread among the crew as the vines lashed out, impaling them with savage ferocity. Shouts of pain and desperation echoed within the confines of the M1128 as the crew fought valiantly against the relentless onslaught.

But it was futile.

One by one, the crew members succumbed to their wounds, their lifeless bodies slumping against the cold metal interior of the tank. The once formidable M1128 now lay silent and still, a grim testament to the overwhelming power of Zayra and her dark magic.

Outside, Zayra surveyed her handiwork with cold satisfaction, her eyes gleaming with malice as she watched the wreckage of the M1128. With a flick of her wrist, she dispersed the thicket vines, leaving behind nothing but devastation in her wake.

As she was about to leave, Zayra heard a faint engine noise from the sky.

Zayra's attention shifted as she heard the faint sound of engines approaching from the sky. Looking up, she saw the unmistakable silhouette of Blackwatch helicopters descending upon the battlefield. From the records Su Xue compiled, she could tell what it was. It was the AH-64 Apache,

Knowing that she couldn't afford to stay and face the approaching threat head-on, Zayra quickly assessed her options. With a flick of her wrist, she summoned a dense dark fog to cloak her movements, concealing herself from the prying eyes of the incoming helicopters.

The Apache pilot, who just saw the thick fog below, smiled.

"You think you can run away with that fog?"

Chapter 321 - 321: The Nuisance
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Can you find her?" the gunner of the Apache asked, his voice crackling over the intercom.

The pilot focused on their instruments, and replied, "Let me use the thermal imaging. It might cut through the fog and give us a better chance of locating her."

The gunner nodded in agreement, adjusting the helicopter's sensors to activate the thermal imaging system. With the flick of a switch, the display screen in front of them shifted to show a heat map of the area below, highlighting any warm bodies amidst the cold fog.

Slowly, the Apache began to hover lower, its sensors scanning the battlefield. The crew strained their eyes, searching for any sign of movement that could indicate Zayra's presence.

Minutes passed in tense silence as they combed through the fog. Still, they can't find Zayra.

"Where did that bitch go?" The gunner demanded.

"Just look into the building, she might be hiding there. Also, more aerial units are one minute out of our position. The moment we find that son of a bitch, we will rain hell—" The pilot paused.

"What? What did you find?"

The pilot's voice crackled over the intercom. "I've got movement in the building to the northeast. Looks like she's trying to take cover."

The gunner adjusted the Apache's position, directing its gaze toward the indicated building. Through the thermal imaging display, they spotted a figure moving cautiously among the shadows within the structure.

"That's her. Let's light her up," the gunner said, determination evident in his voice.

The pilot maneuvered the Apache into position, keeping a steady hover as the gunner aligned the targeting reticle with Zayra's heat signature.

"Locked on target," the gunner announced, fingers poised over the trigger controls.

"Fire at will," replied the pilot.

The gunner unleashed a barrage of rounds from the Apache's mounted weapons, sending streams of bullets tearing through the building's walls. Explosions erupted as the rounds found their mark, ripping through concrete and sending debris flying in all directions.

Inside the building, Zayra scrambled to evade the onslaught, her dark cloak billowing around her as she darted from cover to cover. Despite her best efforts, she couldn't escape the relentless hail of gunfire raining down upon her.

"Ahh…so irritating!" Zayra flicked her fingers, and sharp thorny vines erupted from the ground, snaking their way towards the Apache. The gunner and pilot quickly realized the danger and maneuvered the helicopter to avoid the incoming attack.

"Yeah…keep your eye on her, we can't let her get away when the bomber is already seconds away from the AO," the gunner said.

"Roger that," the pilot replied, maintaining his focus on the thermal imaging display.

Inside the building, Zayra gritted her teeth, frustration evident on her face. "I need to get out of here fast, the Blackwatch is all on me."

***

Meanwhile, in the sky, the B-52 Stratofortress bomber soared towards its target, its payload of precision-guided munitions ready to unleash devastation upon Lin Feng's forces below.

The pilot and crew aboard the bomber maintained a tense silence as they approached the area of operation.

"This is Whiskey Tango 0-2, we are orbiting the AO, requesting target coordinates," the bombardier called out over the radio.

The navigator quickly relayed the coordinates, guiding the bomber towards its designated target with precision.

"Prepare for drop sequence," the pilot ordered.

"Drop!"

B-52 Stratofortress began to release, falling towards their intended target below. The crew watched as the explosives hurtled towards the city below. Seconds later, there were explosions erupting from the impact.

As the bombs hit their target, the shockwaves rocked the Apache, causing it to jolt in the air. The crew watched from a safe distance as the explosions tore through the enemy stronghold, engulfing it in flames and smoke.

"Damn, that's some serious firepower," the gunner remarked. "There's no way she'd be able to survive that."

"Let's see if that bitch is already dead. Blackwatch this is Raptor 5-4, conducting a sweep of the target area."

The Apache dipped lower, cautiously approaching the target area amidst the aftermath of the bombing run. The crew scanned the surroundings, their eyes sharp for any signs of movement.

"Keep an eye out for any hostiles," the pilot instructed.

The gunner adjusted the Apache's sensors, scanning the area for heat signatures or any other indicators of life.

Minutes passed as they conducted their sweep, and the pilot seemingly nodded his head.

"Negative visual on the target," the pilot noted.

"Well, she must have been killed by that bombing run. Let's get out of here and leave this up to the ground crew," said the gunner.

"Hold on…I'm getting transmission from Blackwatch. They said they are two unknown individuals attacking our Northern and Eastern sectors," replied the pilot.

"Unknown individuals only meant one thing, and that is Lin Feng's associates. The one we have been looking for may have been already dead, so let's get our focus on these new threats," the gunner proposed.

The pilot nodded in agreement, already scanning the horizon for any signs of the approaching adversaries. He shifted the yoke slightly, adjusting the Apache's heading toward the reported Northern and Eastern sectors where the unknown individuals were spotted.

"Blackwatch, this is Raptor 5-4. We're redirecting our course to engage the new threats," the pilot radioed in.

"Raptor 5-4, this is Blackwatch. Proceed with caution.

***

Under the rubble, Zayra took a moment to catch her breath, grateful for the cover the vines provided. Despite narrowly escaping the Apache's and bomber's assault, she knew her mission was far from over. With determination burning in her eyes, she focused on her next objective: to slaughter as many Blackwatch forces as possible.

"Vega, Maiden, where are you?" Zayra contacted telepathically.

"I'm fighting the Blackwatch forces, I'm getting pushed back. I need help," Maiden requested.

"Okay…I'll come to you."

As Zayra left her hiding spot, she spotted a silhouette, walking confidently down the street.

"So you are one of Lin Feng's harem huh?" said the man.

Zayra's eyes widened as she recognized the voice.

"Don't tell me…you?"

The man revealed himself, an active plasma blade on his right hand.

"Yeah…you have been a nuisance during my fight with Lin Feng. So I'll kill you this time."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 322 - 322: Extermination Part 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Without further ado, Graves charged forward with the thrusters propelling him powerfully, closing the distance between him and Zayra in a near instant.

Zayra couldn't react. Even though she fixed her gaze on him, Graves was just suddenly in front of her.

She raised her arm up, palm open, unleashing a flurry of razor-sharp vines, instinctively trying to ensnare Graves as he approached. But Graves, anticipating the move, activated his suit's energy shields, deflecting the vines with bursts of concussive energy that sent them recoiling back towards Zayra.

Zayra, undeterred, dug her feet into the earth, drawing deeper into her corrupted powers. The ground beneath them trembled as she summoned a thicket of thorns, rising like a wave to swallow Graves whole. However, Graves, with the agility afforded by his advanced combat suit, launched himself into the air, thrusters blazing, evading the deadly embrace of the thicket.

From above, Graves locked onto Zayra, his suit's targeting systems. He unleashed a barrage of plasma bolts, each one streaking through the air.

Zayra, with a flick of her wrist, conjured a shield of interwoven vines, pulsing with dark energy, absorbing the impact of the plasma bolts…but melted upon contact with the next wave of Graves' attack—a searing plasma blade that sliced through the air, cutting down the protective barrier with ease. The plasma blade, glowing with intense heat, proved too much for Zayra's organic defenses.

As the shield disintegrated, Zayra was forced to leap back, narrowly avoiding a direct hit from the blade.

Graves, seizing the momentum, pressed the attack. He used his suit's thrusters to propel himself forward, closing the gap between them with every second. Zayra, realizing the physical disadvantage she was at, shifted her strategy. Her hands moved in a blur, manipulating the very essence of the corrupted plant life around them.

Vines thicker than before shot up from the ground, aiming not just to entangle Graves but to pierce through the armor of his combat suit.

Yet, Graves was ready. His suit, equipped with advanced sensors, detected the incoming assault. He activated the suit's enhanced mobility functions, dodging the lethal vines with precise, acrobatic movements. Each time a vine shot towards him, he countered with a swing of his plasma blade, cutting through the organic matter as if it were nothing.

The battle became a high-stakes game of cat and mouse. Zayra conjured wave after wave of plant-based attacks, each more deadly than the last, turning the battlefield into a deadly garden of thorns, vines, and spores. Graves, however, met each challenge with the unyielding force of his technology. His plasma blade left a trail of severed plant matter in its wake.

"I can't get him!" Zayra frustratingly yelled. She knew she needed to change her tactics if she stood any chance against Graves' relentless assault. In a swift decision, she shifted her focus from direct attacks to more strategic, environmental manipulation.

The ground beneath Graves began to rumble, and within moments, large spikes of hardened earth, covered in thorny vines, erupted in an attempt to impale him from below.

Graves, however, was not caught off guard. His suit's sensors immediately alerted him to the change in terrain, and with a quick burst of his thrusters, he propelled himself out of harm's way, hovering above the spikes.

"You're going to have to do better than that," he taunted, his voice calm and collected through his helmet's speaker system. She concentrated, drawing on the raw energy of her corrupted power to infuse the surrounding vegetation with a surge of growth and malevolence.

Without warning, the plants around Graves began to animate with a sinister intent, their movements suddenly more coordinated and aggressive. Vines thickened and twisted around each other, forming dense barriers that sprang up around him, attempting to cage him within a living prison.

These barriers were not static; they pulsated with dark energy, making them resistant to physical and energy-based attacks.

Realizing too late the extent of Zayra's command over the corrupted plant life, Graves found himself ensnared, a cage of thick, intertwining vines wrapping tightly around him. The advanced combat suit, designed for flexibility and power, struggled against the relentless grip of nature's bind. Graves, momentarily stalled, assessed his situation with a calm that belied the danger of his predicament.

In a moment of clarity amidst the encroaching panic, Graves activated the plasma cannon built into his suit—a more powerful, albeit energy-consuming, weapon compared to the plasma blade. The cannon, designed for situations requiring significant destructive force, hummed to life, its core glowing with a potent energy ready to be unleashed.

As he prepared to fire, the plants around him began to exhibit an unexpected reaction. The vines, previously a vibrant dark started to turn orange, pulsating with a strange luminescence. It was as if Zayra's creations were responding to the imminent threat of the plasma cannon, or perhaps it was a side effect of the weapon's energy interacting with the corrupted plant life.

Without a moment's hesitation, Graves fired the cannon. The blast was immense, a concentrated beam of plasma energy that hit the caged plant life with such intensity that it instantly began to melt. The orange glow intensified, and the vines dripped away, disintegrating under the heat and power of the plasma.

Freed from his organic prison, Graves stepped out of the puddle of what was once a formidable barrier, his suit steaming but intact. The plasma cannon, having proved its worth, powered down, its energy reserves significantly depleted from the encounter. Graves surveyed the area, noting the scorched earth where the plants had melted.

"Okay, I didn't know you could fight like this," Graves commented. "Earlier you didn't look powerful enough to handle such intensity."

Zayra, catching her breath from the exertion of her powers, looked at the aftermath.

"I couldn't defeat him," Zayra thought to herself. She used every tactic and manipulation of her corrupted plant powers she could muster, yet Graves stood unvanquished. The realization that brute force would not win this battle dawned on her, prompting a strategic retreat within her mind.

"I'm sorry…Master…" Zayra muttered. Accepting her fate.

Graves activated his plasma blade once again, this time, instead of surging forward, he walked forward as if he were death itself. As he neared her, he raised the plasma blade and chopped her head off.

Zayra's head fell to the ground, her headless body falling next to it. Graves knew that Lin Feng's associates could regenerate. So, activating his plasma cannon, he incinerated it down to ashes.

"This is Specter-1, one of Lin Feng's associates is eliminated. Heading over to the secondary location."

"Copy that Specter-1, good kill."

Chapter 323 - 323: Extermination Part 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

inside the state-of-the-art Blackwatch Command Center located in the heart of New Clark, Richard meticulously analyzed the data unfurling across the multitude of screens before him. A vivid green hue, indicative of the spreading influence, was overtaking the digital map of Hong Kong, marking a significant shift in regional control.

Turning to address the President, who was attentively monitoring the situation from his vantage point, Richard reported.

"Mr. President, we've successfully neutralized one of the primary threats. Our focus now shifts to the remaining adversaries, who are currently..." His voice trailed off as he redirected his attention towards a particular feed.

On this screen, a chaotic scene unfolded as his forces engaged a formidable opponent—a woman with the uncanny ability to manipulate shadows, her powers creating a dance of darkness against the assault. "... being effectively contained," Richard concluded, his eyes narrowing as he assessed the evolving confrontation.

"The plan is going smoothly as planned," Richard continued. "We've allocated additional units to reinforce the effort against the shadow manipulator and the ice manipulator. Graves is heading towards Vega's position."

"Thank you for the report, Richard, we appreciate it. It's good to know that our operation is going very well in the early stages," the President responded, a note of satisfaction in his voice. "Keep me informed on Graves' progress towards Vega. The abilities of these manipulators pose a significant risk, and we must handle them before we set Hong Kong as our staging point."

"No problem, Mr. President, we'll handle it," Richard assured and then turned his attention once more to the display screen. "All stations, this is Eagle Actual, press the attack on those two manipulators. I want them…well you know my preferences."

"Yes sir!" the teams on the ground in Hong Kong responded in unison.

***

mAIDEN found herself the focus of an intense military onslaught. The Blackwatch forces, under direct orders from Richard at the Command Center, had escalated their attack, employing a formidable array of military hardware against her.

Maiden, known for her remarkable ability to manipulate ice, created barriers and defenses, but the relentless assault from Blackwatch tested her powers to their limits.

The first wave of the attack came from the Abrams tanks that had been strategically positioned around her last known location. The tanks' main armament, a 120mm smoothbore M256 gun, roared to life, launching armor-piercing fin-stabilized discarding sabot (APFSDS) rounds. These rounds, designed to penetrate the toughest armor, now sought to breach Maiden's ice constructs.

Each impact caused a thunderous explosion, sending shards of ice scattering like glass under a hammer's blow.

Above, an AC-130 gunship, circling like a predatory bird, had locked onto Maiden's position. The gunship's crew unleashed a barrage of 105mm shells. These high-explosive rounds, designed for bunker-busting and softening enemy fortifications, now rained down upon Maiden. The concussive force of the blasts tore through the air, the sound deafening, as plumes of smoke and debris rose into the night sky.

On the ground, Blackwatch infantry moved in, their assault rifles and machine guns at the ready. The soldiers, equipped with the latest in small arms technology, carried M4A1 carbines, a weapon favored for its reliability and adaptability. The M4A1's 5.56×45mm NATO rounds were complemented by the heavier M240 machine guns, firing 7.62×51mm NATO rounds.

The air was filled with the cacophony of gunfire, the rapid staccato of the M4A1s blending with the deeper, more rhythmic pounding of the M240s. The bullets, some tipped with tungsten for enhanced penetration, were designed to defeat personal armor, and now they hammered against Maiden's ice shields, seeking weaknesses, attempting to find or create a breach.

As the battle raged on, Maiden, caught in the crossfire, exerted her abilities to the brink. The relentless barrage from Blackwatch was a clear demonstration of their commitment to neutralize what they deemed a high-value target. She wouldn't last a minute or two here. She wanted to retaliate but should she lower her defenses, she would be obliterated immediately by the overwhelming firepower.

With each passing second, the situation became more dire. Maiden knew she needed to find an escape route or a way to turn the tide in her favor. However, the encirclement by Blackwatch forces was tight, their coordination flawless, leaving little room for error or escape.

As she concentrated, weaving her icy barriers thicker in response to the intensifying assault, Maiden searched desperately for any lapse in the enemy's strategy. The Abrams tanks, with their relentless shelling, and the AC-130 overhead, dropping its payload with ruthless efficiency, presented a near-impenetrable ring of steel and fire.

The infantry, with their disciplined volleys of gunfire, acted as the net, ready to catch any attempt she made to break free.

But due to overthinking, Maiden's defenses crumbled and the rounds got through her defenses. The relentless firepower from the Blackwatch forces overwhelmed her protective ice barriers, which shattered under the combined might of the assault. As the barriers fell, Maiden was exposed directly to the incoming fire.

The final moments were swift. The M240 machine guns' rounds found their mark, causing immediate and incapacitating injuries. The Blackwatch forces pressed on, exhausting all their mags to the point Maiden's body was riddled with holes.

Moments later, Maiden went on her knees and collapsed, her form no longer able to sustain the barrage it had endured. In the eerie silence that followed, the only sound was the crackling of ice melting into pools of water, steaming from the residual heat of the battle.

Richard, observing the outcome from the command center, gave a nod of approval. "Give her a double tap, don't let her regenerate."

The Blackwatch team on the ground moved in cautiously, rifles still at the ready, their eyes scanning for any sign of movement. But Maiden was still; her body seemed almost peaceful in defeat.

When they got close to her body, the Blackwatch forces exhausted their mags again, making sure that they tore her body to pieces so regeneration wouldn't be possible.

After the task was completed, the area was secured, and the Blackwatch forces resumed their task of clearing out Hong Kong district by district. With Maiden down, there's one to go.

Vega.

Graves arrived at the sector where Vega was. And unlike his previous encounter with the Blackwatch forces where the recent battle was Blackwatch victory, this sector was Vega overwhelming the Blackwatch. She is the only remaining primary threat in Hong Kong, defeating her would mean Blackwatch gaining a staging ground for their invasion of China.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 324 - 324: Extermination Part 3
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Graves quickly evaluated the chaotic scene before him. Vega's control over the shadows turned Blackwatch soldiers against each other, ensnaring them in dark tendrils that manipulated their movements, causing friendly fire incidents.

The Abrams tanks, usually the powerhouse of any ground assault, were rendered almost useless, their advanced systems and heavy armor no match for the shadows creeping into every crevice, causing critical system failures.

Understanding the unique challenge Vega posed, Graves switched his suit to a mode designed for electromagnetic interference, hoping to disrupt Vega's shadow manipulation long enough to gain an advantage. He directed the infantry to pull back, minimizing the risk of them harming each other under Vega's influence.

With the ground cleared, Graves initiated his approach, utilizing the thrusters on his suit for speed and maneuverability. As he moved, he launched a series of flashbangs, designed not just to disorient Vega but to momentarily dissipate the shadows with bursts of intense light.

Vega, momentarily caught off guard by the sudden illumination, lost her grip on the shadows. This lapse allowed Graves to close the distance between them, now within striking range. His suit, equipped for close combat, enhanced his physical capabilities, allowing him to engage Vega directly.

The confrontation was straightforward and brutal. Graves relied on the enhanced strength and speed afforded by his suit, delivering a series of targeted strikes aimed at disabling Vega. Vega tried to counter, summoning shadows to bind Graves, but his suit emitted pulses of light, keeping the shadows at bay and rendering her main form of attack ineffective.

Graves's strategy was to wear Vega down, exploiting the brief moments when her control wavered with each flashbang detonation. He observed her patterns, looking for any sign of fatigue or concentration lapse to exploit.

But—as he was throwing punches, he didn't notice that there was a shadow coiling around his left foot, it whipped him around and sent him flying through the air with tremendous force. Graves, caught off guard by the sudden counterattack, found himself unable to correct his trajectory as he hurtled towards a nearby building.

The impact was imminent; his body crashed into the walls with such force that it broke through, sending debris scattering as he tumbled inside.

The building offered no resistance to the force of Graves's unexpected entrance. He found himself lying amidst rubble and dust, his suit's alarms blaring warnings and status updates. Quick to recover, Graves initiated a diagnostic check on his suit while pushing himself up from the debris.

Despite the violent entry, his suit had absorbed the brunt of the impact, preventing any fatal injuries but not without cost. The diagnostics reported minor system malfunctions and a decrease in power efficiency.

Shaking off the disorientation, Graves assessed his new environment. He was inside what appeared to be an old office building, its interior gutted by time and neglect. There was also blood everywhere, possibly from the zombies getting inside and then killing all the civilians.

Graves knew he couldn't linger. Every second spent in recovery was a second Vega could use to consolidate her control over the shadows and potentially launch another attack. He pushed himself to his feet, noting the way his suit whirred and clicked, compensating for the damaged systems as best as it could.

The diagnostics continued to run, flashing occasional warnings on his HUD, but Graves pushed them to the back of his mind. He needed to focus on the task at hand.

He exited the building through the gaping hole that his body had created upon entry. Once outside, Graves didn't hesitate. He activated the thrusters on his suit, propelling himself into the air with a burst of speed.

Hovering above the battlefield, he had a clear line of sight to Vega, who was momentarily focusing on reasserting control over her shadows, directing them against the regrouping Blackwatch forces below.

Graves knew this was his moment to act. He couldn't afford to let Vega regain the upper hand. His suit's targeting system locked onto Vega, a red cursor hovering over her form as he prepared his next move. The plasma cannons, integrated into the suit's arms, hummed to life, charging up with a glow that illuminated the surrounding area with a harsh blue light.

Vega, sensing the imminent attack, attempted to raise a barrier of shadows in defense. The dark tendrils coalesced in front of her, forming a thick wall intended to absorb or deflect any incoming fire. However, Graves's plasma cannons were designed to penetrate through heavy armor and energy shields, making them particularly effective against less conventional defenses.

With a determined grimace beneath his helmet, Graves fired. The plasma cannons unleashed a torrent of searing energy. Vega's shadow barrier, though formidable, was no match for the intensity of the plasma. The energy pierced through the dark wall with explosive force, striking Vega directly.

The impact was devastating. Vega's scream pierced the air as the blast connected, the force of the explosion tearing through her left arm, severing it completely. The limb was obliterated, reduced to ash before it could even hit the ground, leaving Vega reeling from the shock and the sudden, acute loss.

Graves wasted no time capitalizing on the momentary advantage he had gained. With Vega reeling from the loss of her arm, he swooped down swiftly, closing the distance between them in a matter of seconds.

With a swift arm movement, Graves delivered a final blow, his plasma blade slicing cleanly through Vega's neck. The force of the strike was enough to sever her head from her body in one swift motion. As her headless form crumpled to the ground, the shadows she had commanded dissipated, releasing their hold on the battlefield.

"Specter-1 to Eagle Actual, all primary targets eliminated. Moving on to securing Hong Kong," Graves radioed in, his voice firm and decisive amidst the chaos.

"Roger that, thank you for the good news, Specter-1. Now, help our men out there in clearing all the zombies in the AO," came the response from the command.

"Copy that out here," Graves acknowledged briskly, wasting no time. He redirected his focus towards aiding the Blackwatch forces in clearing out the remaining threats in the area.

***

In Blackwatch Command Center.

"You hear that, Mr. President, all primaries are eliminated. Taking all over Hong Kong will be easy from this moment forward," Richard said.

"I agree…but let's not be complacent. Who knows what Lin Feng could throw at us at any moment."

"We have learned that lesson multiple times, it's not going to happen again," Richard assured.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 325 - 325: Reclamation of Hong Kong
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

February 7th, 2024.

The prolonged and arduous engagement with the zombies and Lin Feng's associates in Hong Kong had finally drawn to a close. Under the combined efforts of Blackwatch and the United States Armed Forces, the region was now declared clear of any immediate threats.

Richard, who had been at the helm of the operation throughout its duration, had been operating without pause, driven by the knowledge that unforeseen complications could arise at any moment. Now, with the mission successfully concluded, he allowed himself a moment of reprieve, a deep sigh of relief marking the end of constant vigilance.

The clearance of Hong Kong was not just a military victory; it was a strategic win that opened up numerous possibilities for future operations. The city could now serve as a vital staging point for Blackwatch and the United States Armed Forces, providing a foothold in the region that was crucial for both logistics and operations planning.

In the aftermath, Richard began the process of debriefing and evaluations. It was essential to analyze the operation's execution meticulously, identifying both strengths and areas for improvement. This would ensure that lessons were learned and integrated into future missions, enhancing operational efficiency and effectiveness.

As part of the cleanup operation, teams were deployed to secure sensitive areas, recover equipment, and gather intelligence left behind by Lin Feng's associates. Decontamination was also necessary where soldiers from Blackwatch and the United States armed forces picked up dead bodies of zombies and burned them to prevent any possible outbreaks or contamination.

Search and party rescue roamed major streets and avenues with their megaphone telling all hiding survivors that it was safe to come out and that medical aid and food supplies were available. And there were survivors coming out from dilapidated buildings, houses, underground shelters, and other hiding places.

They emerged slowly, and cautiously, their faces etched with the trauma and weariness of the days spent in fear and uncertainty. The rescue teams met them with blankets, water, and first aid, guiding them to the makeshift relief centers that had been set up in secure zones.

These centers were bustling hubs of activity, where medical personnel attended to the injured, volunteers distributed meals, and counselors offered psychological support.

In the following days, Richard conducted an operation to ramp up the relief efforts. Supplies of food, water, and medical essentials were flown in, while engineers and construction crews worked tirelessly to restore essential services like electricity and water supply. The goal was not just to provide immediate relief but to kickstart the long-term recovery of Hong Kong.

The part of the military base where survivors were gathered was called a survivor's camp. These camps were organized to accommodate the large number of people affected by the conflict. Each camp was equipped with tents, emergency shelters, and portable facilities to ensure the survivors had access to basic living conditions.

Security was a top priority, with patrols set up around the perimeter to provide safety and reassurance to the survivors.

Richard also initiated a registration process within the camps to document all survivors, assessing their needs and any medical attention they required. It will also serve as identification for those who were missing family members, helping to facilitate reunions.

February 18th, 2024. Richard decided to visit Hong Kong. His plane landed at the recently reclaimed airport known as Chek Lap Kok. The airport, which had been temporarily shut down during the height of the conflict, was now operating at partial capacity to facilitate the influx of aid and support teams from the Philippines.

As his plane landed, a ladder was rolled up to the side for him to disembark. Richard stepped out into the mild February air of Hong Kong, greeted by a team of military personnel.

Michael breathed the air and then exhaled it before walking down the ladder. Trailing behind him was Sara and Lisa.

"You said one day you want to visit Hong Kong right?" Richard asked as he glanced over his shoulder, looking over at Lisa.

"Yes brother…but not in its state," Lisa responded, her eyes scanning the surroundings.

Richard nodded, understanding her sentiment. "I know, it's hard to see it like this. But part of our job is to ensure that places like Hong Kong can bounce back, stronger and safer," he explained, leading them towards a waiting vehicle. The drive through the city offered a firsthand view of the extensive damage but also of the ongoing efforts to restore normalcy.

The streets, once scenes of chaos, were now lined with crews clearing debris and repairing infrastructure. Every now and then, they passed by aid distribution points, where volunteers handed out supplies to residents. The operation was massive, coordinated by both local, Blackwatch and United States Forces committed to the city's recovery.

Arriving at the survivor's camp, he was greeted by Graves.

"Sir, welcome to Hong Kong. I hope the place doesn't shock you, it's a lot more different compared to New Clark City."

"We have transformed the New Clark into a city where apocalypse seemed to not happen, we can transform Hong Kong as well," Richard responded, his gaze sweeping over the camp with a critical yet hopeful eye. He could see the vast expanse of tents and makeshift buildings that now housed those who had lost their homes in the conflict.

Graves nodded in agreement. "We've been working non-stop to stabilize the situation here. The camps are just the beginning. There's a long road ahead in terms of rebuilding and ensuring these people can return to a semblance of normal life."

"Well, that's one of the priorities, but the mission still doesn't change. Lin Feng is still out there and we already spooked him out and there's no way he is going to sit there and watch as we grow stronger every day here in Hong Kong."

"I understand the urgency sir, but it's really going to take time for the next mobilization. It's going to be a thousand-kilometer march towards Beijing. This was already laid out perfectly in the operation."

"I know, I'm just saying," Richard said, walking past him. "So how is our military hardware holding up after the operation? I know we pushed our equipment to the limits."

Graves responded, "Most of our hardware is operational. We have a few units undergoing repairs, but nothing that compromises our readiness. The Abrams tanks and the drones took the brunt of it, but our tech teams have been working non-stop to get them back online. We've also requisitioned additional supplies and replacements to bolster our capabilities."

Richard nodded, pleased with the update. "Good, I want a full inventory report by tomorrow. I want to know what machines we need to acquire for the upcoming operation."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 326 - 326: Prelude to the Actual Invasion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Beijing, China.

Lin Feng tapped his foot as he looked into his system.

"So Vega, Maiden, and Zayra are dead huh? And they have successfully secured Hong Kong. What great news, they now have a staging ground for their invasion."

"This is a great concern for us, master," Su Xue stepped forward, expressing her concern. "With the Blackwatch and the United Forces now in Hong Kong, it's only a matter of days before they get here."

"I know…but they'll have to work their way up here," Lin Feng said confidently. "Before they could even reach Beijing, there won't be any forces left for them to stop me."

"A Fabian strategy would be useful in our situation, master," Su Xue proposed.

"Fabian strategy?" Lin Feng repeated, unfamiliar with the word.

"Fabian strategy is a military strategy where we avoid direct confrontation with the enemy, focusing instead on wearing them down through a war of attrition and delaying tactics," Su Xue explained. "It means avoiding large battles and instead engaging in smaller, more manageable conflicts that sap the enemy's strength and morale over time."

Lin Feng nodded thoughtfully. "A strategy of exhaustion... Yes, that could work in our favor. The longer we can delay them, the more time we have to strengthen our defenses and plan our countermeasures."

"Exactly, master. We can utilize guerrilla tactics, hit-and-run attacks, and sabotage operations to disrupt their supply lines and communication," Su Xue elaborated. "It will force them to spread their forces thin, making them vulnerable and less effective."

"I see," Lin Feng opened his system as he began purchasing a huge number of mutated zombies. Hunter, Goliaths, Flyers, and Juggernauts. All those four for now since they are the cheaper and somehow the most important forces available to him

He'd summon city to city, creating obstacles and diversions as the Blackwatch and United Forces advance. But as he was doing that, he realized something.

"Wait…our enemy is a system user, same as mine. Their losses could be easily replaced by Richard. So would the Fabian strategy work here?"

"Master…his summons are only humans, they are no match against your summons, which are monstrous beasts that have supernatural strength and agility. Even if they are inside a tank or a helicopter, they'd be no match if our beast can get to them."

"Well…that's a good point. Anyways, master, should they get here, and we are on the verge of losing, get out of the city. I've already arranged several safe houses around the country for you to use as hideouts," Su Xue continued.

"Get out if we are losing?" Lin Feng scoffed. "I don't think so. We'll end this once and for all. It's going to be me dying or the Blackwatch."

"So this is going to be our final battle huh?" Su Xue noted.

"Yes, Su Xue, this is going to be our final battle. It's been dragging on for so long. Eliminate Blackwatch, and then our vision for this world will be fulfilled. If not, then well Richard is going to be a hero for the world," Lin Feng concluded.

Su Xue nodded, her expression serious. "Understood, master. I will ensure all preparations are in place. Our forces will be ready for their advance."

***

Meanwhile, in Hong Kong.

Blackwatch forces constructed a platform in the middle of the military base where Blackwatch and the United States Armed Forces are based. Thousands of Blackwatch troops and United States Forces were standing side by side, all heads high and eyes fixed on the platform where Richard was about to address them.

As Richard took his steps towards the podium, the crowd fell silent, their attention honed in on him. Reaching the podium, Richard scanned the crowd, his gaze reflecting a mixture of pride and resolve. He then began to speak.

"Everyone, we have conquered Hong Kong, and have set up a staging ground for our next operation to save humanity from extinction. Of course, it is without its loss. A lot of men died, Blackwatch and US soldiers. This wouldn't be possible without their sacrifices.

Let their bravery and dedication serve as a beacon for us all as we continue this fight," Richard solemnly stated, his voice steady, ensuring every word carried weight across the attentive audience.

He continued, "Our next phase is critical. We are going to march cities after cities, reclaim them, and make them free from zombies and Lin Feng's influence until we reach Beijing. We will make sure that no zombies will ever tread upon this soil."

Richard paused for a moment, letting the gravity of his words sink in among the assembled forces. Then, with renewed vigor, he continued his speech, his voice projecting determination and hope.

"We stand on the brink of a new dawn. Each step we take towards Beijing is a step towards the future—a future where fear no longer holds dominion over our lives. A future where families can live without the dread of losing loved ones to the darkness that has engulfed our world. We are the architects of that future, and it is within our power to build it."

He gestured towards the soldiers, embodying the unity and strength that had brought them this far. "Look around you; see the resolve in your comrades' eyes. It is the same resolve that has seen us through the darkest of times. It is the resolve that will see us to victory. Our path is fraught with peril, but we have faced peril before and emerged stronger."

Richard's words stirred a palpable sense of purpose among the troops. He spoke of the sacrifices made, not just by those in uniform but by civilians who had endured unimaginable horrors. "We owe it to the fallen, to the survivors, and to future generations to end this nightmare.

We will march, fight, and, if necessary, lay down our lives to secure a future free from the shadow of Lin Feng and his minions."

The air was charged with energy as Richard reached the crescendo of his address. "So, let us march forward with unwavering resolve. Let our march be relentless, our spirit indomitable, and our purpose clear. We march for freedom, for peace, and the dawn of a new era. Together, we are invincible. Together, we will reclaim our world!"

The response was immediate and thunderous, a chorus of voices rising in agreement and determination.

The assembled soldiers, united by Richard's invigorating speech, cheered and whistled, their morale boosted to new heights.

"Let's march to Beijing!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 327 - 327: Invasion: Shenzhen
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
