The campaign to reclaim territories from the clutches of chaos brought the Blackwatch and United States forces to their first major target beyond Hong Kong: Shenzhen, China. This sprawling city, once a bustling hub of innovation and commerce, had fallen into despair, its streets overrun by the undead.

To initiate their operation, the forces employed a barrage of firepower, with HIMARS (High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems) and artillery howitzers leading the charge. Their objective was clear: to cleanse the streets of Shenzhen from the zombie menace that plagued them.

As the first light of dawn broke over the city, the artillery units took their positions, their barrels aimed with precision at the densest concentrations of the undead. A thunderous roar followed as the HIMARS and howitzers unleashed their deadly payload, sending a series of explosive projectiles arcing across the sky.

The salvo descended upon Shenzhen, igniting a series of powerful explosions that rocked the city's foundations.

The impact was devastating. Buildings that had stood as silent witnesses to the apocalypse now bore the scars of this new onslaught. The explosions tore through the undead ranks, sending limbs and debris flying. For a moment, the city was alight with a deadly fireworks display.

In the wake of the artillery's fury, infantry and armored units began their advance. Armored vehicles, including Abrams tanks and Bradley fighting vehicles, rolled through the streets, their turrets swiveling to engage any threats. Behind them, squads of Blackwatch and United States soldiers moved in formation, their rifles at the ready.

They were the cleanup crew, tasked with eliminating any zombies that had survived the initial bombardment.

The urban environment of Shenzhen presented a unique challenge. Every street and alley could hide potential threats, and the dense urban sprawl made progress slow and perilous. But they were helped by air support from above. AC-130, A-10 Warthog, Apache helicopters, and drones provided crucial support from the skies.

The AC-130 gunships, with their devastating firepower, circled overhead, raining down bullets and shells on identified zombie clusters. A-10 Warthogs swooped in with precision, their GAU-8 Avenger rotary cannons shredding through the undead with ease. Apache helicopters provided close air support to the ground troops, targeting any significant undead threats that could potentially halt the advance.

Drones, both for surveillance and combat, offered a significant advantage, giving the commanders a bird's eye view of the battlefield. They identified key zombie congregations and relayed real-time data back to the command center, ensuring that artillery strikes were both precise and effective.

This aerial reconnaissance allowed the ground forces to navigate through the city more safely, avoiding larger hordes and focusing on clearing strategic areas critical for the city's reclamation.

As they were moving into the deeper part of Shenzhen, they encountered something.

"That's a Goliath," Richard said, looking at the screen. Those humongous monsters with shields and an arm blade that is as tall as a building.

Richard, understanding the challenge ahead, immediately coordinated a response strategy. "Focus all available firepower on that Goliath," he ordered.

Abrams tanks adjusted their aim, targeting the giant with armor-piercing rounds, while Apache helicopters maneuvered for a direct assault, their Hellfire missiles locked onto the monstrous adversary.

The battle that ensued was intense. The Goliath charged, its shield scraping the paved streets of Shenzhen, causing debris to scatter as it moved toward the Blackwatch and United States forces with terrifying speed.

Helicopters and the AC-130 gunship worked in tandem, focusing their firepower on the Goliath's more vulnerable spots. The Apache helicopters darted around the Goliath, avoiding its attempts to swat them from the sky. They fired Hellfire missiles at the joints of its arm and the areas not covered by its shield, aiming to cripple its mobility and offensive capabilities.

The AC-130, circling above, had a broader view of the battlefield. Its crew targeted the Goliath with a combination of 105mm cannon fire and precision-guided munitions, exploiting every opening provided by the Apache helicopters' attacks.

The relentless barrage from the air was complemented by the ground forces' heavy artillery, which pounded the Goliath from a distance, creating a continuous stream of explosions around it.

Despite the Goliath's formidable strength and armor, the combined firepower began to take its toll. Sparks and debris flew as its armor was pierced by the concentrated attacks. The beast roared in defiance, trying to push forward, but the damage to its limbs slowed its advance significantly.

Infantry units, taking advantage of the Goliath's weakened state, moved in closer, using rocket-propelled grenades and anti-tank weapons to target any exposed or damaged areas.

As the Goliath finally fell, its massive body crashing to the ground with a thunderous impact, a brief silence fell over the battlefield. The soldiers, panting and scanning the surroundings for more threats, and there was. Alpha Hunters and Juggernauts.

They rushed towards their position, easily preying on the infantries of the Blackwatch and the United States forces. Their small arms weren't effective on the tough hide of the monsters and they were quickly overwhelmed by the sheer force and ferocity of the attackers.

Juggernauts, with their immense size and strength, hurled soldiers into the air with terrifying ease, their bodies landing with sickening thuds. Alpha Hunters punched through the ranks of soldiers, their powerful blows sending bodies flying into walls with such force that they left indents and cracks upon impact.

The sudden appearance of these formidable opponents caused momentary chaos among the Blackwatch and United States forces.

"Send in the heavy cavalry," Richard directed. Immediately after his command, tanks rolled in, forming a defensive line between the remaining infantry and the advancing Juggernauts and Alpha Hunters. Their main guns thundered, sending powerful shells that exploded upon impact, causing significant damage to the Juggernauts.

Meanwhile, Bradley Fighting Vehicles, supporting the tanks, opened fire with their 25mm chain guns, targeting the quicker and more agile Alpha Hunters. The rapid-fire capability of the Bradleys proved effective in slowing down the Hunters, piercing their hides with a barrage of armor-piercing rounds.

The air support, never far behind, swooped in once again. This time, the Apache helicopters focused on the Juggernauts, launching Hellfire missiles at any of the behemoths that got too close to the Blackwatch and United States forces' defensive line.

The intense battle raged on, with the Blackwatch and United States forces managing to hold their ground thanks to the timely arrival of the "heavy cavalry." The combined efforts of ground and air forces began to turn the tide, with the number of Juggernauts and Alpha Hunters dwindling under the sustained assault.

One hour later, mutated zombies were no longer seen in Shenzhen, they were all eliminated.

"Shenzhen captured!" Richard announced.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 328 - 328: We are Getting Near
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Two months later. April 26th, 2024.

The Blackwatch and the United States Forces had fought major battle after battle, conquering cities of China from the undead. So far, casualties are high with Blackwatch suffering eight hundred while the United States Forces five hundred. Yet they still pressed on.

The Joint Force arrived in the city of Tianjin, just 140 kilometers away from Beijing. They are getting closer.

Meanwhile, at the Joint Force Command Center in Tianjin. Richard was checking his system.

[User: Richard Gonzales

Health: 500/500

Age:22

Available Skill Points: 0

Level: 64

Skills: Weapon Proficiency Level 3, Melee Proficiency Level 3, Driving Proficiency Level 2, Tactical Mastery Level 3, Pilot Mastery Level 3. Vitality Level 3, Enhanced Perception Level 2.

Experience points: 21,516,248,112/25,761,235,790

Gold balance: 6,025,616,411,511]

"Six trillion gold coins huh?" Richard read his gold balance. It was indeed in six trillion gold coins. The reason why the Blackwatch has been able to push deeper into the territory of China is that all the losses are replaceable. Tanks, Aircraft, and even troops. Of course, he is cautious not to show this power to their partner, the United States as he doesn't want to be scrutinized.

As he was in thought, Mark stepped forward.

"Sir, enemy movement detected. The numbers are 15 Goliaths, 231 Alpha Hunters, 200 Juggernauts, 50 Hydra, 80 Cerberus, 1,000 Flyers, 5 Alpha Gryphons, and four Lin Feng's associates."

Hearing that report, Richard raised a brow. "That's quite a number Lin Feng is throwing at us. Does he want to end this war here in Tianjin?"

"If he would do that, he would have joined the advancing forces. But according to our intelligence, Lin Feng was not seen among their ranks," Mark responded, tapping on the digital map displayed on the large screen in front of them. The map showed real-time movements, with the enemy forces highlighted in red as they approached Tianjin.

"Then it's a diversion," Richard concluded, his mind racing through the possibilities. "He's trying to draw us out, make us commit here so he can hit us elsewhere. Or perhaps, he's testing our defenses before making his real move."

Mark nodded, "It's possible, sir. Our scouts have reported that the enemy forces seem to be moving with more coordination than before. It's as if they're being directly commanded."

Richard leaned back in his chair, pondering their next move. "Prepare our defenses. I want the HIMARS and artillery units on standby to strike as soon as the enemy is within range. Deploy drones for continuous surveillance. We need to keep an eye on their movements at all times."

"Understood, sir. And what about our reserves?" Mark inquired, referring to the additional forces held back for emergency deployment.

"Keep them ready but hidden. We'll only deploy them if absolutely necessary. I don't want Lin Feng to know the full extent of our capabilities," Richard ordered. "Also, inform the United States Forces' commanders about the situation. We need to coordinate our defense to withstand this assault."

As Mark went to carry out his orders, Richard turned his attention back to his system. With a few taps, he began reallocating resources, ensuring that frontline units had all they needed for the imminent battle. Despite the overwhelming numbers they were about to face, Richard felt a surge of confidence.

The battles they had fought had honed their tactics and teamwork, and every victory brought them closer to their ultimate goal.

***

In the city of Tianjin, above the sky, 1,000 Flyers were flying in formation, quickly becoming the focal point of the city's defenses. As they descended upon Tianjin with menacing speed, the Blackwatch and the United States forces' anti-air defenses, primarily the Counter-Rocket, Artillery, and Mortar (C-RAM) systems, sprang into action.

These highly sophisticated defense mechanisms, designed to track and destroy incoming aerial threats, began firing at an intense rate, sending a storm of bullets toward the incoming Flyers.

The sky was ablaze with the light of tracer rounds, each burst from the C-RAM systems creating a deadly barrier against the invading Flyers. The precision of these systems was unmatched, tracking the fast-moving targets with unerring accuracy. However, the Flyers darted through the sky with unnerving agility, dodging the barrage of bullets with surprising finesse.

Their objective was clear: to breach the defenses and wreak havoc among the ground forces.

Despite the ferocity of the anti-air barrage, several Flyers managed to penetrate the defensive perimeter, swooping down towards the troops with their talons outstretched. Soldiers scrambled for cover, firing their rifles and machine guns at the descending threats.

The situation escalated quickly as the Flyers, undeterred by the gunfire, grabbed soldiers with their powerful talons, attempting to carry them off into the sky.

The battle was chaotic, with the ground forces struggling to repel the aerial attackers. The C-RAM and mobile anti-air units continued to fire relentlessly, bringing down several Flyers in spectacular explosions. Yet, the persistence of the Flyers proved challenging, their numbers and agility allowing them to pose a significant threat despite the intense defensive fire.

Not even attack helicopters such as the Apache and the drones were immune to the challenges posed by the Flyers. They'd swarmed around the helicopter and used their sharp talons to scratch the body, creating dents and attempting to break through. Pilots performed evasive maneuvers, desperately trying to shake off the aggressive Flyers. Despite their best efforts, it was just futile.

Helicopters crashed and exploded upon impact.

Due to that, the Blackwatch has decided to ground all aircraft except from Fighter Jets and Gunships to minimize the risk of losing more aerial assets to the Flyers. This tactical shift forced a change in their approach to the battle.

The ground forces intensified their use of anti-aircraft weapons, deploying more mobile anti-air units equipped with surface-to-air missiles and heavy machine guns specifically designed to engage low-flying targets.

The streets of Tianjin turned into a battleground, with Blackwatch and United States forces setting up defensive positions around key points of the city. Barricades and improvised explosive devices were placed strategically to slow down the advance of the ground enemy forces, while sharpshooters took up positions in high buildings to provide cover for their comrades below.

Meanwhile, Richard and Graves, along with ten special forces, stood behind them, all wearing the Titan's Guard Mark II.

"It's time boys," Richard said.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 329 - 329: Let's Go Boys Part 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Richard, Graves, and ten members of the Special Forces split up, each heading to strategically important locations around Tianjin to intercept enemy forces. Richard's target was a lone Goliath advancing from the east toward the city's outer defenses.

As Richard approached, the Goliath detected his presence, reacting with a swift upward swing of its massive arm blade in an attempt to take him down. With quick reflexes, Richard maneuvered sharply to the left, narrowly avoiding the deadly swipe. He quickly retaliated, aiming his plasma cannon at the Goliath.

The shot was precise, hitting the Goliath directly in its left eye, causing significant damage but not enough to stop it completely.

The Goliath put its hand over to its eye visibly in pain. But Richard felt no remorse or feelings toward it. So he swooped in, activating his plasma blade and then slicing the Achilles tendon of the Goliath to further impair its mobility. The Goliath roared in pain and frustration, its advance significantly hindered by the precision of Richard's strike.

Now limping and partially blinded, the Goliath became less of a threat to the city's outer defenses but still posed a danger that needed to be eliminated.

Richard circled above, analyzing the situation. The Goliath, now severely weakened, attempted to continue its assault on Tianjin, driven by whatever dark impulse or command it was under. Richard knew he had to finish this quickly to prevent any potential damage it could still inflict.

He communicated with Graves and the Special Forces team, updating them on the situation and requesting additional firepower to ensure the Goliath's swift elimination. Within moments, support arrived in the form of heavy artillery, positioned at a safe distance but with a clear line of sight to the Goliath.

On Richard's command, the artillery units opened fire. The barrage was relentless, and under the concentrated firepower, the Goliath could no longer maintain its stance. Each hit took a toll, further weakening the structure of the behemoth until it finally collapsed, unable to withstand the onslaught.

Richard looked atop its head, and there it was slivering health bar. It's not yet dead. He landed on its chest and prepared for the final blow. With his plasma blade still activated. He walked over to the right, where its heart was located. The moment his foot touched down, the Goliath let out a weak roar, a last attempt to intimidate or perhaps a reflex of its fading life force.

Richard didn't hesitate; he knew the importance of finishing this quickly to prevent any resurgence. Positioning his plasma blade directly above the Goliath's heart area, he thrust downward, piercing through the thick hide and into the core of the beast.

The effect was immediate. The remaining life force of the Goliath flickered out as the plasma blade disrupted its vital systems. Richard watched as the health bar vanished completely, confirming Goliath's termination. He withdrew his blade and stepped back.

With the Goliath neutralized, he tried joining the rest—No, an Alpha Hunter leaped from the right, its hand extended forward as if it were going to maul him. However, Richard's perception skill and the predictive feature of the suit allowed him to simply lean backward, dodging the incoming attack with a fluid motion. The Alpha Hunter's claws swiped through the air, missing Richard by mere inches.

In the brief moment the Hunter was off-balance from its missed strike, Richard capitalized on the opportunity. He activated his plasma blade once more, turning swiftly to deliver a counterattack aimed at the Hunter's midsection.

The blade made contact, cutting through the Hunter's flesh with ease. The creature howled in pain, recoiling from the attack, but it was far from defeated.

It rose up and then unleashed a flurry of swipes in rapid succession Richard, relying on his enhanced perception, dodged each attack, moving with a speed and agility that seemed almost superhuman. The dance between predator and prey was intense, with the Hunter relentlessly pursuing Richard, who skillfully evaded and countered when opportunities presented themselves.

Despite Richard's superior agility, the confined space of the battlefield and the Alpha Hunter's persistence began to narrow his options. The Hunter, learning from its initial failed attempts, started to anticipate Richard's movements, forcing him to adopt a more defensive posture.

Realizing that a prolonged battle could attract more enemies or drain his resources, Richard decided to change tactics. He feigned a moment of vulnerability, baiting the Hunter into committing to a powerful lunge.

As the Hunter took the bait and surged forward, Richard used the momentum against it, sidestepping at the last moment and using his plasma blade to deliver a critical strike to the Hunter's weakest part.

The impact of the blade was significant, cutting deep into the Hunter's torso and severing vital pathways. The creature staggered, its movements becoming sluggish as it tried to comprehend the severity of its wound. Richard didn't give it a chance to recover. He pressed the advantage, delivering a series of rapid strikes with his plasma blade, targeting the weakened areas of the Hunter's body.

Finally, Richard's blade found its way to the Hunter's heart, effectively ending the threat it posed. The Hunter collapsed, its body going limp as the life faded from its eyes. Richard stood over it for a moment, ensuring it was truly down, before deactivating his plasma blade and scanning the area for more immediate threats.

With the immediate danger neutralized, Richard quickly checked his surroundings for any signs of additional enemies or any of his team members in need of assistance.

"Hmm? Alpha Gryphon huh? Don't worry, I'll be there," Richard said to himself, noticing the distress signals and the reported location of an Alpha Gryphon wreaking havoc on another part of Tianjin. Knowing the devastation that such a creature could cause if left unchecked, Richard didn't waste any time.

He activated his thrusters, propelling himself into the air with a burst of speed, heading straight for the location of the Alpha Gryphon.

As he approached, the Alpha Gryphon came into view, its massive wingspan casting a shadow over the ground below. The Gryphon spotted Richard approaching and let out a piercing screech

Richard assessed the situation quickly. The Gryphon was agile in the air, but he knew its size and strength would be its downfall if he could disable its ability to fly. With this strategy in mind, Richard closed the distance, dodging the Gryphon's talon swipes.

Coming in close, Richard aimed his plasma blade at one of the Gryphon's wings, slashing at the base where it would cause the most damage. The blade cut through the thick hide and muscles, severely injuring the wing and rendering it useless. The Gryphon screeched in pain and anger, its flight now erratic as it struggled to maintain altitude with one wing compromised.

Not giving the Gryphon a moment to recover, Richard targeted the other wing, repeating his earlier maneuver. The second wing was also severely damaged, and without the means to stay airborne, the Gryphon plummeted to the ground, crashing with a heavy thud that shook the nearby area.

With that, Richard called an artillery strike to where the Alpha Gryphon landed, and moments later, multiple explosions erupted.

"Good thing this was easy. If we are having difficulties, then we won't stand a chance against Lin Feng."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 330 - 330: Prelude to the Final Battle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After another grueling day of combat, Richard, Graves, and their team of ten Special Forces managed to neutralize the threats surrounding the strategic location of Tianjin. The cost of this victory was steep for the Joint Forces, which comprised both Blackwatch and United States Forces. The operation resulted in three hundred personnel either killed or wounded in the line of duty.

Additionally, the battle took a significant toll on their equipment, with ten aircraft and twenty land vehicles either destroyed or rendered inoperable due to the intense confrontations.

The aftermath of the battle left the forces assessing their losses and strategizing their next moves. The city of Tianjin, now relatively secure, served as a critical foothold for the Joint Forces, but the victory was bittersweet.

The high casualty rate underscored the ferocity of the opposition they faced and the challenges that lay ahead in their mission to reclaim territories overrun by the undead and Lin Feng's forces.

Richard, amidst the sobering reality of their situation, convened with Graves and the surviving members of the Blackwatch Chief of Staff and the President of the United States.

"As you all can see, the battle from here is starting to get intense. Based on our intelligence reports, Lin Feng is not moving away from Beijing. He is waiting for us there," Richard said.

"So when are you going to mobilize the army to take out Lin Feng?" President Barry asked.

"I'm thinking about tomorrow at 0600 HRS. Depending on the resistance, we might reach there within a day or two," Richard responded.

Graves added, "We've been reinforcing our positions and replenishing our supplies as quickly as possible. But we need to be strategic about our next move. Rushing into Beijing without adequate preparation could result in unnecessary losses."

President Barry nodded in understanding. "I trust your judgment on this, Richard. The support from our side will continue. Let us know what resources you need, and we'll make it a priority to get them to you."

"Everything is taken care of, Mr. President, don't worry on the logistical side. The Blackwatch has unlimited resources at its disposal," Richard assured.

"Yeah…and I'm beginning to notice that. Like where is your arsenal coming from?" President Barry raised an eyebrow, his curiosity is evident. "Your resources seem... extensive."

Richard met the President's gaze with a calm demeanor. "Let's just say we've managed to secure a unique supply line that ensures our operations can continue without hindrance. The specifics are, for the moment, classified, but I assure you, it's all above board and in the interest of our mutual goal."

President Barry, though still intrigued, decided not to press further. "Alright, Richard. I'll take your word for it. Just ensure that these resources are used wisely. Now I'm wondering about the civilians we have rescued up to this point. Are there still Chinese citizens alive in different cities?"

"Yes, along the way we picked up some survivors who have been hiding out, waiting for a rescue," Richard replied, shifting the topic to a more pressing concern. "We've established safe zones in the areas we've secured, providing them with medical aid, food, and shelter. Our goal is not only to defeat Lin Feng and his forces but also to protect and assist the civilians caught in this conflict."

Graves added, "The number of survivors is less than we hoped, but each life saved is a victory in itself. We're coordinating with local leaders we have selected and volunteers to ensure these people can start rebuilding their lives, even as we continue our campaign."

President Barry nodded, his expression somber. "Make sure those survivors know they're not forgotten. The United States is committed to aiding in the recovery process, once Lin Feng is dealt with. The world needs to see that we're not just liberators but also builders and healers."

Richard acknowledged the President's words with a firm nod. "We'll make sure of it, Mr. President. Our teams are working around the clock to ensure the safety and well-being of every civilian we encounter."

"As for the assault on Beijing," Richard continued, "we're finalizing our tactical plans. We expect heavy resistance, but we're prepared for whatever Lin Feng throws at us. Our forces are motivated and ready to end this threat once and for all."

"Okay…I think I have heard what I need to hear, gentlemen, that would be all. Do tell me if there's anything specific you require from our side," President Barry concluded, signaling the end of the meeting.

Richard and Graves stood up, acknowledging the President's support with a nod. "Thank you, Mr. President. We'll keep you updated on our progress," Richard said, offering a respectful salute before cutting off the transmission.

"Okay. Tomorrow will seem to be our final battle against Lin Feng," Richard said, turning to Graves.

"Yes sir…it's been eight months since the zombie apocalypse, and I can already see the light at the end of the tunnel," Graves replied.

"I can't wait to be back to Sara's side and be with my budding child," Richard responded, his tone softening at the mention of his family.

"Speaking of which, when is Sara due?" Graves inquired, showing a rare moment of personal interest amidst the ongoing crisis.

Richard paused for a moment, calculating. "Well, we found out she was pregnant back in January, so if I make an educated guess... she should be due around late August or early September," he said, a mix of anticipation and worry in his voice.

"That's soon," Graves noted, "You must be excited... and anxious."

"Yeah, excited to meet my child, anxious about the world they'll be born into. But first, we've got a war to end," Richard replied, his gaze returning to the task at hand. "Let's make sure we wrap this up before then. I don't want Lin Feng or this apocalypse to be part of my child's future."

Graves nodded in agreement, "We'll make sure of it, sir. Let's give it our all tomorrow."

The two shared a moment of mutual understanding before turning their attention back to the preparations for the upcoming battle. The conversation about Sara and the baby was a brief respite from the relentless pressure of their mission.

As the night deepened, the command center buzzed with activity, everyone focused on their duties with renewed determination. Richard, too, threw himself into the final preparations, ensuring that every detail was accounted for, every contingency planned for.

And then five o'clock in the morning. All troops were ready for deployment for their last march in China.

"This is it, Beijing," Richard muttered. "I'm going to kill you, Lin Feng."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 331 - 331: It's Beginning
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Lin Feng stood atop the highest building in Beijing, his eyes scanning the horizon where the forces of Blackwatch and the United States Armed Forces were making their final approach. Distant rumbles of artillery and the whir of aircraft can be heard overhead.

"Interesting indeed," Lin Feng repeated. The strategy he had employed, a war of attrition designed to deplete the invaders' resources and will, had ultimately failed. The Joint Forces had proven more resilient and resourceful than he had anticipated.

Su Xue, standing by his side, her expression stoic yet resolute, nodded in agreement. "It's now or never, Master. This is where it's going to end," she affirmed her loyalty to Lin Feng unwavering even in the face of the impending battle.

Lin Feng's response was a roar, a declaration of his confidence in their victory. "And I intend to see myself emerging victorious out of it!" he proclaimed. Without hesitation, he extended his arms forward, channeling his dark powers. Black tendrils of biomass erupted from his hands, weaving together in a complex pattern to form a formidable shield.

This barrier was his first line of defense against the onslaught of artillery shells that Blackwatch had begun to rain down upon their position.

The sky was alight with the fiery arcs of incoming projectiles, each one aimed with deadly precision at Lin Feng's stronghold. But as they collided with the biomass shield, they were either deflected or absorbed, their explosive force nullified by the dark energy that Lin Feng commanded.

Despite the effectiveness of his shield, Lin Feng knew this was only the beginning. The Joint Forces would not be deterred so easily. They had come too far and sacrificed too much to be stopped by a single barrier, no matter how powerful. He glanced at Su Xue, a silent acknowledgment of the gravity of the situation.

They were about to face the full might of an enemy that had fought its way across China, undeterred by the hordes of undead and the traps Lin Feng had laid in their path.

As the initial barrage of artillery fire subsided, Lin Feng could hear the distant roar of engines, signaling the approach of ground forces. Tanks, armored vehicles, and legions of soldiers were advancing, their movements coordinated.

He glanced at the sky and saw an AC-130 approaching, its silhouette menacing against the backdrop of a smoke-filled sky.

From its arsenal, the AC-130 unleashed a barrage of 105mm shells, each one aimed directly at Lin Feng's position atop the building. Alongside the heavy shells, the rapid fire of its 25mm guns added to the onslaught, creating a relentless stream of projectiles aimed at breaking through Lin Feng's defenses.

The black tendrils of Lin Feng's barrier twisted and contorted, straining under the impact of the AC-130's firepower. Each shell that struck the barrier caused it to shimmer and crack, pieces of the dark energy flaking away like chipped paint. Lin Feng stood firm behind his weakening shield, his concentration unwavering as he poured more of his energy into maintaining the barrier.

Despite the barrier's formidable appearance, it was clear that it was starting to give way under the continuous bombardment.

Su Xue watched anxiously, aware that even Lin Feng's formidable powers had their limits. "Master, the barrier won't hold much longer at this rate," she cautioned, her voice tense with concern.

"Do not falter, for this is easy," Lin Feng calmly said as he controlled a spear-shaped biomass not far from him, aimed towards the AC-130.

He launched it at supersonic speed towards the AC-130. The spear tore through the air, its speed so great it was barely visible, headed straight for the aircraft.

As the spear impacted, it sliced cleanly through one of the wings of the AC-130, the force of the attack causing a catastrophic failure. Metal groaned and shrieked as the wing was severed, sending the aircraft into an uncontrollable spin.

The crew inside scrambled, their training kicking in as they attempted to stabilize the plane and assess the damage, but the loss of a wing made it an impossible task. The AC-130 began to descend rapidly, a trail of smoke marking its path toward the ground.

Lin Feng watched as the aircraft fell, a grim satisfaction on his face. Moments later, the AC-130 exploded mid-air, the shockwaves from it could be felt from where Lin Feng stood. The destruction of the AC-130 was a significant blow, but Lin Feng was well aware that this was only a part of the extensive arsenal at the Joint Forces' disposal.

He turned to Su Xue, signaling that they needed to prepare for the next wave of attacks.

"Their air power is formidable, but not invincible," Lin Feng remarked. "They will come with more, and we must be ready."

Su Xue nodded, her expression determined. "What are your orders, Master?"

"We fortify our position here. Enhance the barriers and set up more defenses. They will not find us easy prey," Lin Feng instructed. As he spoke, he gestured with his hands, manipulating the dark energy around them to strengthen their defenses.

Meanwhile, on the ground, the advancing Joint Forces were regrouping after witnessing the destruction of the AC-130. Commanders were issuing orders, adjusting their strategies in real-time to compensate for the loss and to anticipate Lin Feng's next move. The tanks halted momentarily, adjusting their positions, while the infantry took cover, waiting for the next phase of the assault.

"Okay, it's time that we pay this man a visit," Richard said, glancing at Graves.

"For real this time? We are going to beat the shit out of him?" Graves asked.

"Yeah…let's hope that our arsenal is enough…"

"Speaking of arsenal, I'm still waiting for one," Graves said. "We are going to deploy them in this battle right?"

"Of course…speaking of which, they are probably above us right now," Richard said.

***

Meanwhile, in the airspace of Beijing. Ten C-17 Globemaster flew in formation, and their rear cargo ramp opened, signaling the deployment phase. From each of the massive aircraft, Mecha robots, began dropping into the battlefield.

As they landed, the ground beneath them trembled. The Mecha units, once on the ground, quickly shed their parachutes and activated their combat protocols. Each robot, standing several meters tall, was a formidable force, equipped with plasma cannons, missile launchers, and reinforced steel armor capable of withstanding heavy firepower.

Their presence on the battlefield was a surprise from the United States forces. They never anticipated that one coming.

"There's no way they are going to miss this fight," Richard said.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 332 - 332: Lin Feng in Action
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Lin Feng watched as the Mecha robots, brought into the battlefield by Blackwatch, began their advance. He quickly recognized these new additions as significant threats.

"So he's got the Mechas now?" Lin Feng noted, acknowledging the escalation in Richard's capabilities.

Su Xue, observing alongside him, pointed out the implications. "This changes everything. They've got enough firepower to take Beijing now," she remarked, highlighting the gravity of their situation.

Lin Feng, however, didn't waver. "They might have their machines, but they're forgetting I'm Lin Feng. I'm not just another one of my summons; I can't be beaten by their tech. I'll show you," he confidently stated, dismissing the idea that technological superiority could determine the outcome of their confrontation.

With that, Lin Feng jumped from the building, landing with such force that it caused the ground to shake, sending debris flying and creating cracks on the surface.

As Lin Feng dashed across the roads, his path soon intersected with a squad of Blackwatch soldiers. Positioned behind makeshift barricades, they immediately recognized the threat and opened fire with their assault rifles. The bullets, however, proved ineffective against Lin Feng. As each round struck him, it simply passed through or was absorbed, his body regenerating any damage almost instantly.

Unperturbed by the gunfire, Lin Feng advanced towards the soldiers with a menacing determination. One particular soldier, standing in Lin Feng's direct path, continued to unload his rifle into the advancing figure. The relentless barrage of bullets seemed to slow Lin Feng for a moment, but his advance remained unstoppable.

Realizing his rifle was out of ammunition, the soldier quickly switched to his sidearm, aiming directly at Lin Feng's head in a desperate attempt to stop him. Each bullet from the pistol met the same fate as those from the rifle, proving futile against Lin Feng's regenerative abilities. The soldier's efforts were in vain, as Lin Feng closed the distance, his presence alone enough to instill fear.

"Do you think a gun could defeat me?" Lin Feng asked the soldier while ignoring the other soldiers who kept firing at him. "So why still use it?"

The moment he asked that Lin Feng grabbed the soldier by the neck and hoisted him up. The soldier pulled out a tactical knife and repeatedly stabbed the arm that was holding him, but the blade didn't even leave a scratch on Lin Feng's skin. Instead, the knife's edge dulled with each futile attempt.

Lin Feng, observing the soldier's desperate and ineffective efforts, simply shook his head. "Your bravery is commendable, but bravery alone wouldn't make much of a difference."

Saying that coldly, Lin Feng crushed the man's neck, and a snapping sound of a bone was heard amidst the chaos of battle. Dropping the lifeless body to the ground, Lin Feng turned his attention to the remaining soldiers who stood frozen in fear, witnessing their comrade's demise.

With a determined stride, Lin Feng closed the distance between himself and the soldiers. Bullets continued to rain down upon him, but he paid them no mind as they bounced off harmlessly or were absorbed into his regenerating form.

One by one, Lin Feng dispatched the soldiers with swift and decisive movements. Some attempted to flee but found themselves trapped by the surrounding wreckage or intercepted by Lin Feng's relentless pursuit. Others stood their ground, only to meet the same fate as their fallen comrades.

It was too easy for Lin Feng. However—before he could turn his eyes in another direction, a powerful blow struck him from the side, sending him flying

across the battlefield. Lin Feng crashed into a nearby building, the impact shaking its foundations and sending shards of glass raining down around him.

Groaning with the force of the blow, Lin Feng quickly regained his bearings, pushing himself up from the rubble. As he scanned the area, he spotted the source of the attack: a towering Mecha robot, its metallic form bristling with weaponry.

Grimacing with determination, Lin Feng leaped back into action. Ignoring the pain coursing through his body, he charged toward the Mecha, his eyes blazing with defiance.

The Mecha, sensing Lin Feng's approach, unleashed another barrage of plasma cannons firepower, missiles streaking through the air towards him. But Lin Feng was undeterred. With lightning-fast reflexes, he dodged and weaved through the onslaught, closing the distance with each passing moment.

He already knew how to deal with mechas. If they are piloted, well the easiest way is to take down the pilot inside. Lin Feng focused his efforts on finding a weakness in the Mecha's armor, searching for any opening that would allow him to access the cockpit.

Dodging the Mecha's attacks, Lin Feng maneuvered himself closer to his target. With each evasive move, he analyzed Mecha's movements, searching for patterns and vulnerabilities.

As another volley of missiles streaked towards him, Lin Feng seized the opportunity to strike. Using the debris scattered across the battlefield as cover, he darted toward Mecha.

Reaching the towering machine, Lin Feng leaped onto its armored hull, clinging to its surface as he searched for a way inside. Spotting a small panel near the Mecha's neck joint, he quickly pried it open, revealing the cockpit within.

Without hesitation, Lin Feng launched himself inside, his fists striking out as he targeted Mecha's pilot. Caught off guard by Lin Feng's sudden intrusion, the pilot attempted to defend himself, but Lin Feng's relentless assault left him no room to maneuver.

Killing the pilot of the mecha, Lin Feng incapacitated the pilot, causing the Mecha to lose its control. As the machine's movements became erratic, Lin Feng seized the opportunity to disable its power source, rendering it immobile.

Emerging from the cockpit victorious, Lin Feng surveyed the battlefield making sure that there wouldn't be any surprises like the one earlier.

Just as Lin Feng was about to assess the situation further, the ground beneath him trembled with the unmistakable roar of an approaching Abrams tank. With its imposing silhouette cutting through the smoke and debris, the tank rolled into view, its cannon trained directly at Lin Feng.

Reacting quickly, Lin Feng leaped out of the Mecha's cockpit, narrowly avoiding the incoming barrage of cannon fire. The explosive shells tore through the battlefield, sending shockwaves reverberating through the air.

Knowing he needed to destroy that tank, Lin Feng rushed forward and once he was at a suitable distance, he jumped into the sky and then landed on top of the tank. He stomped his foot down onto the tank's turret with all his might, denting the thick armor plating and momentarily disabling the cannon. The crew inside scrambled to react, but Lin Feng was already one step ahead.

Using his incredible strength, Lin Feng tore open the hatch, exposing the panicked crew within. Without hesitation, he reached in and pulled them out one by one, swiftly incapacitating them to prevent any further resistance.

Dealing with them easily, he could finally venture into the deeper part of Beijing where Blackwatch control is solid. He wanted to show the Blackwatch that he won't be defeated here.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 333 - 333: The Final Clash Begins
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Meanwhile, on the opposite side of the city. Richard received a report from his communication channel.

"Eagle, Lin Feng is rampaging in the northern sector and has taken a lot of casualties. The mechas that we have sent to hold him were eliminated."

"This is Eagle, I copy. I'll head there to the location immediately and confront him," Richard responded.

"Copy that Eagle, sending you the coordinates of Lin Feng, you should see it on your heads-up display…now," Mark said.

The holographic screen flickered to life and a map with a red pulsating dot indicating Lin Feng's location appeared before Richard's eyes.

"Graves, you'll handle the fort here," Richard said, turning his head towards Graves who just crushed a zombie skull with his hands.

"Me? You are going there alone sir?" Graves asked, tilting his head to the side. "Confronting him alone is dangerous."

"I know…but who is going to handle all of this should we all confront Lin Feng?" Richard pointed, glancing around them where mutated zombies such as Goliaths, Juggernauts, Hunter, Hydra, literally every zombie that they came across since the start of the apocalypse, are here.

Graves clicked his tongue, acknowledging Richard's concern. "Understood, sir. I'll hold the fort here and coordinate with the remaining units to maintain control of the situation," Graves replied sternly.

With a nod of appreciation, Richard turned and activated the thrusters of his suit, soaring into the sky toward Lin Feng's location. As he flew over the city, Flyers swarmed along his way.

Richard activated his plasma blade and cut his way through the flock of Flyers, their screeches echoing in the air as they fell to the ground. Pressing onward, Richard maintained his course toward Lin Feng's location, determined to confront the threat head-on.

As Richard flew over the city towards Lin Feng's location, the devastation wrought by the ongoing battle came into full view. Strewn across the streets were the remains of both Blackwatch and United States soldiers, their uniforms torn and bloodied, their weapons abandoned amidst the chaos.

Immobilized and destroyed military hardware littered the landscape, including tanks, armored personnel carriers, and the lifeless husks of mechas.

He shifted his focus back on the pulsating dot indicating Lin Feng's location on his heads-up display. With grim determination, Richard pressed on, navigating through the city's war-torn streets.

As he approached the designated coordinates, Richard's senses sharpened his every instinct on high alert. He could feel the tension in the air, a palpable sense of anticipation as he drew closer to his target.

Suddenly, a loud explosion rocked the city, jolting Richard from his thoughts. He glanced towards the source of the blast and saw a towering column of smoke rising into the sky. Without hesitation, he redirected his course toward the scene of the explosion, knowing that Lin Feng must be nearby.

As he neared the source of the disturbance, Richard spotted movement amidst the debris—a figure clad in black, his presence unmistakable. It was Lin Feng, his aura radiating power and menace as he surveyed the destruction he had wrought.

"Oh…look who we have here, Iron Man. I don't know what to call you but your suit looked like it was from the movies," Lin Feng said, glancing over his shoulder.

"Lin Feng…" Richard spoke, his voice amplified by the suit's

speakers to ensure it carried over the sounds of destruction around them. "Your rampage ends here."

Lin Feng turned to face Richard, a smirk playing on his lips. "So we meet again? Leaders to leaders. So you want to end this war right here and now?"

"That is what I intend to do," Richard said, activating his plasma blade.

Lin Feng knew that Richard's suit was the real deal. He had to use his demonic form just so he could keep up with it. This is not the time for him to show off, if he wants to win this war, he'll have to give it his all from the start.

With that, Lin Feng transformed. Black swings sprouted on his back, an arm blade from his right arm, and twin horns protruding from his forehead as he unleashed his demonic form.

Richard narrowed his eyes, he could certainly feel that Lin Feng was stronger than before, but Lin Feng was still in the middle of his transformation.

Well, he is not going to wait for that. Michael activated his plasma cannon and fired a beam of plasma energy directly at Lin Feng, aiming to disrupt his transformation process and catch him off guard. The blast struck Lin Feng squarely, sending him staggering backward with a roar of pain.

"Due to your edginess that dripped on your every word, I have assumed that you have watched animes or read light novels, where most of the time, they would wait for the other character to transform fully before attacking. But this wasn't one of those stories. In battle, every second counted."

After saying that, Richard dashed forward, the plasma blade at the ready. Lin Feng finished the transformation and radiated a powerful shockwave that blasted every creature and debris in its path. Caught off guard by the sudden shockwave, Richard was thrown backward, his suit's thrusters struggling to stabilize him in the air.

Despite his best efforts to maintain his balance, the force of the shockwave was too great, and Richard crashed into a nearby building with a resounding impact.

As the dust settled around him, Richard shook off the daze and quickly assessed his surroundings. The building he had crashed into was partially collapsed, debris littering the ground around him. His suit's sensors indicated no significant damage, but he could feel the strain of the impact coursing through his body.

Gritting his teeth, Richard pushed himself to his feet and activated his thrusters once more, propelling himself back into the air. Their blades met with a spark, with each of them struggling to gain the upper hand in their fierce melee.

With every clash of their blades, a resounding shockwave echoed through the air, rattling the nearby structures and sending debris flying in all directions. Richard and Lin Feng were locked in a brutal melee, their blades flashing and sparking with each strike.

Lin Feng could feel the weight of Richard's blade, it was getting heavier and stronger. And he can't seem to hit him. Every time an opening was made, he would go for it but only to be misled. It seemed that every attack he made was anticipated by Richard.

"Why I can't land a hit on him?!" Lin Feng cursed inwardly as he continued to clash with Richard, frustration mounting with each failed attempt to strike his opponent.

With a sudden burst of speed, Lin Feng feigned an attack to Richard's left, aiming to draw his attention away from his true target. As Richard moved to intercept the feint, Lin Feng swiftly changed direction, launching a surprise attack at Richard's exposed flank.

But Richard was ready. Anticipating Lin Feng's deception, he pivoted gracefully, deflecting the blow with a well-timed parry. With a swift counterattack, Richard drove Lin Feng back.

"Lin Feng…this is where you will die!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 334 - 334: It's Far From Over
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Lin Feng…this is where you will die!"

Richard said that with conviction as he charged forward again, blade pointed forward, aimed at Lin Feng's head.

Lin Feng reacted quickly, raising his forearm to block the attack.

The plasma blade pierced through his thick arm, stopping the blade from ever reaching his eyes, only millimeters away from his eyeball.

Richard had been in this situation before where his blade would get stuck in the enemy. Good thing, that the plasma blade is not like an ordinary blade that they have to yank or pull out to remove. Instead, Richard disengaged the plasma blade's power source, causing it to retract back into its housing with a faint hum.

With a swift motion, he withdrew the blade from Lin Feng's forearm, leaving a smoking gash in its wake.

As Lin Feng recoiled from the strike, Richard wasted no time, he activated the plasma blade and raised it once more, ready to deliver the finishing blow. But before he could strike, Lin Feng opened his mouth wide and let out a deafening roar, unleashing a shockwave of energy similar to Violet.

Richard crashed into a nearby building, the impact rattling the suit ever so slightly and leaving him momentarily dazed. As he struggled to regain his bearings, he could feel the slight throbbing ache of his injuries coursing through his body.

But there was no time for Richard to dwell on his injuries. As he pushed himself up from the rubble, his gaze locked on Lin Feng, who stood across the street.

Before Richard could react, Lin Feng made his move. With a swift motion, he switched his arm blade into a claw-like appendage and drove it into the ground. Instantly, thick spears of hardened earth erupted from the ground, hurtling toward Richard with lethal intent.

Reacting quickly, Richard activated the thrusters on his suit and propelled himself into the air, narrowly avoiding the deadly spears as they shot past him with a deafening roar. With adrenaline coursing through his veins, Richard focused on evading Lin Feng's relentless assault while formulating a plan of attack.

As he soared through the air, he called the Blackwatch.

"Eagle, this is Blackwatch Command. Aerial bombardment authorized. Grid number 35162, 152516. Fire at will, danger close confirmed."

"Copy that Eagle, sending rounds, danger close confirmed," came the swift response from Blackwatch Command.

High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems (HIMARS) and M777 howitzers, positioned strategically around the urban battlefield, prepared to unleash their fury. The HIMARS is a lightweight mobile launcher, capable of firing guided rockets and Army Tactical Missile Systems (ATACMS). Meanwhile, the M777, a 155mm towed howitzer was being readied to provide heavy artillery support.

As the coordinates were confirmed, the HIMARS launched its GPS-guided rockets with a thunderous roar. The rockets streaked across the sky, leaving a trail of smoke behind them. Simultaneously, the M777s adjusted their barrels, taking into account wind speed and the earth's curvature, and fired their shells.

The sound of the howitzers firing was deafening, the powerful recoil pushing back against the earth as each shell was sent hurtling toward the designated grid.

Lin Feng, sensing the imminent danger, ceased his attack and looked up just as the first of the rockets and shells began to impact. Explosions rocked the area, sending shockwaves through the streets and shattering windows.

Richard, still airborne, watched as smoke and debris filled the air. He knew that this damage wouldn't be enough to kill Lin Feng, so he remained cautious and wary of any movements that might indicate Lin Feng's next move.

The battlefield, now obscured by smoke and littered with debris, presented a challenge for both visual and sensor-based tracking. Richard's suit, equipped with advanced thermal and motion detectors, scanned the area for any sign of Lin Feng's heat signature or movement.

Amidst the smoke, there was a glowing red light that caught Richard's attention. It was Lin Feng, his body starting to emit a bright, pulsating red glow. Richard knew this was not a good sign. In the world of enhanced combatants, a glowing signal often indicated a critical state, either as a last resort or a dangerous buildup of energy.

As Richard watched, the glow intensified, illuminating the smoke-filled air with a sinister light. Lin Feng's injuries, rather than debilitating him, seemed to trigger this response, a dangerous mechanism embedded within him, perhaps a self-destructive countermeasure for situations exactly like this.

Richard realized the immediate danger. If Lin Feng were to explode, the resulting blast could level blocks, endangering countless lives, specifically the Blackwatch soldiers and the United States forces.

Yes, he may be able to replace them but that's not the case here. It's still lives!

Without hesitation, Richard re-engaged his suit's thrusters, propelling himself toward Lin Feng at full speed.

As he approached, Feng's body reached its critical point. The glow became blinding, and Richard braced for the explosion, shielding his face with his arm.

But instead of an explosion, there was a sudden release of energy upwards into the sky, creating a towering pillar of light that pierced the clouds. The shockwave was so great that Richard was sent flying again, crashing into buildings like dominoes.

As Richard regained his composure, his primary concern shifted back to Lin Feng.

"I'm starting to hate him," Richard said, opening up the displays of the user interface of his suit.

[System diagnostics]

Structural Integrity: 72%

Energy Levels: 63%

Weapon Systems: Operational.

Thrusters: Operational.

Sensors: Nominal.

Communications: Operational.

Critical Warnings:

Minor hull breaches detected.

GPS signal is intermittent.

Energy reserve optimal.

He checked his HUD and saw the GPS was not working properly. It was flickering, struggling to maintain a stable connection amidst the electronic interference caused by Lin Feng's energy surge. Without reliable GPS data, Richard had to rely more heavily on his suit's other sensors and his own instincts—.

Then those instincts picked up something. He heard tremors from below, and then a warning flashed across his HUD.

[Warning, enemy movement detected beneath!]

The alert caught Richard's attention immediately, snapping him back to the present danger. He realized that Lin Feng was far from finished.

He looked to the ground—and Richard's world turned upside down as a direct uppercut from Lin Feng landed squarely on Richard's jaw, sending him rocketing upwards into the air. The force of the blow was so intense that it sent him up to the sky.

As Richard struggled to regain control in mid-air, his suit's systems worked overtime to stabilize his trajectory and provide a damage assessment. The impact had not only compromised the suit's structural integrity further but also jolted the internal systems, causing sporadic malfunctions.

"It's far from over!" Lin Feng roared.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 335 - 335: Replacing Suit
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Richard shook his head, trying to clear the intense pain that throbbed at his temples. His vision was a blur, each blink an attempt to stabilize the world around him. A pounding headache echoed through his skull. The disorientation was temporary, but the urgency of the situation gave him no luxury to dwell on the discomfort.

He forced his focus back to the task at hand, aware that any delay could be costly.

He looked down—only to see Lin Feng already inches away from him. Lin Feng threw another punch but Richard's precognition skill and the predictive features of the suit allowed him to move his head to the side, narrowly avoiding the attack.

The missed punch by Lin Feng created a brief opening, and Richard, capitalizing on this momentary lapse, countered with a swift jab aimed at Lin Feng's midsection.

Lin Feng staggered back, caught off guard by the sudden counterattack. Richard didn't waste the opportunity. With his vision gradually clearing and his focus sharpening, he advanced towards Lin Feng, ready to press his advantage. The suit's diagnostics ran in the background, continuously updating him on his status and the integrity of the system after the impact.

Despite the throbbing pain in his head, Richard's training and the suit's enhancements worked in tandem, providing him with the agility and strength needed to face Lin Feng head-on. He launched a series of punches. Some landed while others were evaded or blocked by Lin Feng, who was quickly regaining his composure.

Richard threw a haymaker right into Lin Feng's face, but Lin Feng caught his fist in midair. With a swift motion, Lin Feng twisted Richard's arm, leveraging his grip to hurl Richard toward the ground with incredible force. The impact was so severe that it created a crater in the road, with Richard lying at its center.

The collision with the ground sent a shockwave of pain through Richard's body, exacerbating his headache and blurring his vision once again. He struggled to rise, feeling the weight of his body and the suit as he attempted to push himself out of the crater. The diagnostics from the suit flashed warnings and critical alerts, indicating damage and the need for immediate attention.

"I can't defeat this man alone," Richard said to himself as he saw Lin Feng barreling down to the Earth with his feet directly at him, ready to deliver what could be a devastating blow.

Acting on instinct and the urgent need for self-preservation, Richard rolled to the side, narrowly escaping the impact point where Lin Feng's feet smashed into the ground, creating another crater where Richard had just lain.

Scrambling to his feet, Richard realized the battle was reaching a critical point. His suit, although advanced, was not invulnerable, and the continuous strain of combat was taking its toll. The diagnostics continued to flash red, signaling that his suit's repair systems were struggling to keep up with the damage being inflicted.

Fortunately, there is a way for him to fix that. Luckily, there was an A-10 Warthog circling above. Lin Feng tapped into the communication channel of the A-10 Warthog and commanded.

"This is Eagle, relaying to you my position. Call for fire, target is in front of me," Richard instructed.

"Copy, Eagle. Target acquired. Beginning to run now. Keep your head down," came the pilot's response

As the A-10 made its approach, the distinct sound of the Avenger spooling up filled the air. Richard sprinted for cover, diving behind a large piece of debris just as the cannon began to fire.

The rounds tore through the air, targeting the area where Lin Feng was positioned. The ground erupted, dust and debris flying as the cannon's rounds impacted with explosive force.

Enraged, Lin Feng's wings spread, and with a powerful leap, he propelled himself into the air, his body seemingly defying gravity as he pursued the A-10 Warthog.

The A-10 throttled its engine to the maximum, attempting to evade Lin Feng's relentless pursuit.

"Eagle, we've got an unexpected situation. Target is airborne and in pursuit."

"Let him chase you," Richard ordered. "Just buy me the time I need and I'll get him out of the sky."

Acknowledging Richard's plan, the pilot continued to push the A-10 to its limits, drawing Lin Feng further away from the battlefield and giving Richard the necessary time to replace his suit.

He opened his inventory and then plucked the Titan's Guard Mark II out from it. After picking it out, he removed the suit he was currently wearing and tossed it aside to the ground.

Richard quickly donned the new armor. The suit's systems came online, rapidly conducting diagnostics and syncing with Richard's biometrics. The HUD lit up, displaying vital stats, weapon systems status, and a tactical overview of the surrounding area.

The pain from his injuries was still there, but the suit's medical subsystems began administering pain management protocols, allowing him to focus more clearly on the task at hand.

His eyes flickered, finding Lin Feng and the A-10. It zoomed in on their high-speed chase, analyzing possible interception trajectories.

"There," Richard said, readying his plasma cannon to the direction of the interception trajectory. He calculated the speed, distance, and Lin Feng's erratic movements, compensating for all variables. The plasma cannon's whirring reached a crescendo, indicating it was fully charged and ready to fire.

Richard didn't hesitate. He locked onto the projected path of Lin Feng's pursuit and fired. The plasma bolt, a brilliant streak of blue energy, shot across the sky, bridging the distance with incredible speed. It was a long shot, given Lin Feng's agility and the ever-changing dynamics of aerial combat. Yet, the bolt hit its mark, exploding on impact and sending shockwaves through the air.

Lin Feng's advance was abruptly halted. The force of the explosion sent him tumbling through the sky before he managed to stabilize himself, his wings beating furiously to regain control. The shock and surprise were evident in his movements; this was a scenario he hadn't anticipated.

"Focus on me," Richard taunted.

Lin Feng seemingly heard Richard's taunt, his gaze shifting to locate the source. Despite the distance and the disorientation from the blast, Lin Feng's resolve didn't waver. He adjusted his trajectory, now heading straight for Richard with increased speed.

Richard prepared for the incoming assault and then contacted someone.

"Eagle to Specter-1, I might need your help over here."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 336 - 336: 2v1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Graves, who just handed one Alpha Hunter, received a transmission from Richard.

"Hmm…I can go, but the rest has to stay here," Graves responded, tossing the dead Alpha Hunter to the side. "The situation here is still worse than we imagined. Mutated zombies are everywhere and our forces are being stretched too thin…Eagle? Eagle are you still there?"

"I'm here!" Richard's voice came through the comm, strained and interspersed with the unmistakable sound of combat. Graves could hear the faint noise of blades clashing and the howling winds, and the earth sundering.

"Give me your Eagle coordinates, so I can respond to your location immediately," Graves requested, already moving to a safer spot to avoid drawing attention from nearby threats. "I need to make sure I'm headed in the right direction."

"I'm sending you the coordinates," Richard said, and moments later, Graves' HUD displayed a flashing indicator marking Richard's position.

Graves absorbed this information with a nod. "I got your location now. I'm making my way to you. Keep yourself in one piece until I get there."

As Graves navigated through the devastated cityscape, he could see the evidence of the fierce battles that had taken place. The destruction was widespread, with buildings reduced to rubble and streets torn apart. There were also craters.

He encountered pockets of mutated zombies along the way, but Graves dealt with these threats efficiently, conserving his strength and energy for the confrontation ahead.

The closer Graves got to Richard's location, the more intense the resistance became. He started encountering more mutated zombies. But he dealt with them swiftly one by one until he arrived in the vicinity of Richard's coordinates. The sound of combat grew louder, a clear indication that he was getting close.

Upon reaching the area, Graves quickly assessed the situation. Richard was exchanging blow by blow against Lin Feng. He watched closely, waiting for the right time to strike.

And that time arrived when Lin Feng thrust his arm blade. Graves' thrusters propelled him forward like a bullet, closing the distance between them in a fraction of a second. He tackled Lin Feng, using his momentum to force the assailant away from Richard. The impact sent Lin Feng staggering back, momentarily caught off guard by the sudden intervention.

"Graves took you long enough," Richard managed to say, panting heavily from the exertion of the fight.

"No time for small talk. Let's finish this," Graves replied, his attention fixed on Lin Feng, who had regained his footing and was now glaring at them.

"Ahh…the two of you again. Good, because I don't have to find the other one once I finish either one of you."

Graves, standing slightly ahead, signaled to Richard with a quick nod, a silent agreement on their strategy. Richard moved to flank Lin Feng, forcing him to split his attention. Graves took advantage of this diversion, launching a series of rapid strikes aimed at testing Lin Feng's defenses.

Lin Feng countered with a series of swift, powerful counterattacks, his arm blade slicing through the air. Graves, anticipating this response, used his agility to dodge and weave, creating openings for Richard to exploit. Richard, seizing these moments, attacked with a combination of high and low strikes, aiming to further disorient their foe.

Graves darted towards Lin Feng, throwing a powerful right hook aimed at Lin Feng's jaw. Lin Feng, quick to react, parried with his forearm, redirecting the force. Seizing the moment, Richard, now at Lin Feng's side, launched a low kick towards Lin Feng's knees, attempting to destabilize him.

Lin Feng, maintaining his balance, countered by spinning, utilizing the momentum to unleash a sweeping kick aimed at Richard's head.

Richard ducked under the kick, rolling forward to regain his stance quickly. Graves, capitalizing on Lin Feng's temporary focus on Richard, advanced with a barrage of jabs, forcing Lin Feng to retreat. Lin Feng, his back momentarily to Richard, found himself trapped between the two.

Richard, now behind Lin Feng, grabbed Lin Feng's shoulder, pulling him backward while simultaneously driving his knee into Lin Feng's back. Lin Feng, caught off guard, stumbled forward into Graves's awaiting uppercut, which connected solidly, sending Lin Feng reeling.

Lin Feng, now visibly frustrated, roared and unleashed a burst of energy, creating a shockwave that pushed both Graves and Richard back.

Graves, recovering from the shockwave, noticed Lin Feng charging with his arm blade glowing ominously. He signaled to Richard, and both sprang into action. Richard, drawing Lin Feng's attention, feinted to the left, then rolled to the right, narrowly avoiding Lin Feng's blade.

Graves, using his suit's enhanced speed, circled to Lin Feng's blind spot, delivering a powerful kick to Lin Feng's side, exploiting the brief opening created by Richard's feint.

Lin Feng, now off-balance, struggled to defend against the coordinated assault. Graves and Richard, sensing victory, intensified their attack. Graves launched a flurry of punches, each blows more forceful than the last, while Richard, weaving around Lin Feng's flailing counterattacks, found an opening, driving a solid punch to Lin Feng's stomach.

As Lin Feng doubled over, Richard and Graves simultaneously delivered a synchronized strike, a combination of a knee from Richard and an elbow from Graves to the head, effectively knocking Lin Feng to the ground.

"Let's end this," Richard said, activating his plasma cannon.

"With pleasure," Graves repeated, his own weapon systems coming online. They both aimed at Lin Feng, now trying to rise, his movements sluggish and pained.

But just as they were about to fire, Lin Feng, with a defiant roar, transformed his arm blade into a massive hammer fist. He smashed it into the ground beside him, the impact sending a shockwave of debris and force rippling outward.

The sudden eruption of power caught Richard and Graves off guard, lifting them into the air. They were thrown back, their aim disrupted, and their weapons discharged harmlessly into the sky. As they struggled to regain control, Lin Feng used this moment to scramble to his feet and poke something before him.

Richard realized what he was doing. Lin Feng is using the system.

And he was right, multiple red magic circles appeared on the ground, and there were mutated zombies, all of them were Juggernauts. And not only that, Lin Feng's associates seemed to have arrived as well.

"We are going to need a battalion, sir," Graves said.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 337 - 337: 2v2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Richard's gaze was locked onto the formidable adversaries arrayed before them.

"Yeah, we might need reinforcements," he acknowledged with a grim tone, "but I'm not convinced they'll afford us the luxury of time to even call for backup."

Despite the dwindling odds, a resolve hardened within him. "Still, our only gambit might be to signal for help. It's a long shot, but it's all we've got," he declared, his eyes moving with practiced ease to activate his suit's distress beacon.

The moment the beacon was engaged, it emitted a rhythmic, pulsating signal.

"Okay, distress signals are now emitting, the command should receive it anytime now," Richard said.

"So what's the plan, sir?" Graves asked.

Richard assessed the situation. There are over 20 Juggernauts and one Lin Feng's associate. The associate has long golden blonde hair and a beautiful appearance, looking straight into his eyes with contempt. He had never seen that woman before, or he had seen her but he can't remember exactly when and where.

But he could feel a strange aura around her like she was not like the rest of Lin Feng's associates, she was the real deal.

If they were already struggling against Lin Feng, the presence of a battalion of Juggernauts and a formidable woman only compounded their predicament.

"The key here," he began, his voice steady, "is not to defeat them all. We need to survive until our backup arrives."

Graves, understanding the direness of their situation, nodded in agreement. "And if the backup doesn't arrive?" he asked, a hint of concern threading his voice.

"We make sure it does," Richard replied with a determination that left no room for doubt. He glanced once more at their enemies, who were now preparing to make a move.

"You two are done exchanging your last words?" the golden blonde-haired woman taunted from the center of their adversaries, her arms transforming into lethal blades with a metallic sheen and red pulsating veins.

Richard and Graves exchanged a brief look, a silent agreement passing between them. There was no time for prolonged strategies or hesitation.

"Remember my name, Su Xue, master's second-in-command. I will not forgive you for hurting my beloved master," Su Xue declared, her voice laced with venom as she launched herself toward Richard and Graves with a speed that seemed to blur her movements.

Richard quickly responded, "Graves, focus on the Juggernauts. I'll handle h—"

Richard couldn't complete his words as Su Xue was already in front of him with a blade drawing near his neck.

He actuated his plasma blade and swept up in a defensive arc, blocking Su Xue's attack just in time. The collision of their weapons produced a shower of sparks, illuminating their determined faces. Richard pushed back with force, creating distance between them.

As Richard prepared for another onslaught, he caught a glimpse of something horrifying in his peripheral vision. The Juggernauts were now stationary, their massive forms being overtaken by an eerie tendril biomass. This living mass seemed to originate from Lin Feng, enveloping the Juggernauts and drawing them into itself like a predatory entity consuming its prey.

Richard's eyes widened as he noticed Lin Feng's health bar, which was growing at an unprecedented rate, fueled by biomass assimilation.

Before he could warn Graves, Su Xue's blade was on a downward swing towards him again. Richard, with a quick sidestep, avoided the attack, barely escaping a fatal strike.

"Graves! Lin Feng is regenerating! You have to stop him!" Richard shouted.

Got it!" he yelled back, firing off a plasma cannon towards Lin Feng. Multiple explosions erupted around Lin Feng as the projectile struck him. The biomass tendrils were now limping around, visibly damaged by the assault.

It did stop the regeneration but Lin Feng already consumed more than enough to join the fight.

Lin Feng rushed forward to Graves and it was now a 2v2 situation.

As Lin Feng charged towards Graves, Richard found himself locked in a battle of speed and precision against Su Xue. Despite her agility, Richard noted that Su Xue's attacks, while fast, didn't carry the same devastating impact as Lin Feng's. This observation became key to his strategy.

Richard decided to use his environment to his advantage, maneuvering around the debris and fallen Juggernauts to create obstacles for Su Xue, slowing her advance and forcing her into a position where the lack of force behind her strikes became a significant disadvantage.

Each time Su Xue launched herself at Richard with her blade arms extended, he would counter with his plasma blade, using the momentum of her attacks to guide and flip her off balance. This tactic not only prevented her from landing a solid hit but also gradually wore her down, as she had to exert more energy to recover from each misstep.

Meanwhile, Graves was employing a different strategy against Lin Feng. Knowing he couldn't match Lin Feng's speed, Graves relied on his suit's enhanced durability and firepower. He set up a barrage of plasma cannon fire, creating a perimeter of explosions around Lin Feng to restrict his movements.

Whenever Lin Feng managed to close the distance, Graves would engage his suit's shock pulse, a close-range defensive mechanism designed to repel attackers. The shock pulse sent Lin Feng staggering back, buying Graves precious seconds to reposition and continue his ranged assault.

The two battles, though separate, were intrinsically linked, with each pair trying to outmaneuver and outwit their opponent. Richard and Graves communicated through quick, coded signals, coordinating their attacks to ensure they didn't inadvertently cross paths or interfere with each other's strategies.

Su Xue, growing increasingly frustrated with her inability to land a decisive blow on Richard, attempted a high-risk maneuver. She feigned a direct attack, then, at the last moment, vaulted over Richard, aiming to strike from behind. But Richard, anticipating her move, spun around, his plasma blade meeting her mid-air. The force of the collision sent her crashing to the ground, momentarily dazed.

Seizing the moment, Richard moved in to incapacitate Su Xue but was suddenly forced to divert his attention as Lin Feng, having momentarily broken free of Graves's barrage, launched a surprise attack towards him. Richard barely managed to deflect Lin Feng's strike with his plasma blade, the force of the impact sending vibrations through his arms.

Graves, seeing Richard in peril, adjusted his aim and fired a concentrated plasma shot at Lin Feng, hitting him squarely in the back. The impact of the shot propelled Lin Feng forward, away from Richard.

Richard sighed a relief, it was close. The two of them regrouped, standing side by side.

"I got your back sir, "Graves said.

"And I got yours," Richard responded.

And they received a transmission from the Blackwatch command.

"Eagle, this is Blackwatch Actual, we just received your distress signal, how can we help?"

"First of all, I need an airstrike at our position and send everyone here! We are facing Lin Feng and his subordinate," Richard replied.

"Copy that Eagle."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 338 - 338: Enraged
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"So, should we swap?" he asked, glancing over at Richard with a smirk that was palpable even through his combat suit's visor. It was clear that Graves felt a surge of confidence after their narrow victory against Lin Feng and Su Xue.

Richard, checking the integrity of his plasma blade for any damage from the intense battle, couldn't help but chuckle at Graves's suggestion. "Swap, huh? And give you all the fun of dancing around with speedsters while I get pummeled by heavy hitters?" he replied, his tone playful yet tinged with the fatigue that comes from facing death head-on.

Despite the jesting, Richard's eyes were scanning the figures before them. Lin Feng and Su Xue were already on their feet.

"Airstrike would be coming at any moment so braced for impact," Richard warned.

Graves nodded in acknowledgment of Richard's plan, his expression serious as he mentally prepared for the next phase of their operation.

"As long as it takes those two down for good, I'm all in," he said, adjusting the settings on his suit to maximize his defensive capabilities in anticipation of the airstrike.

Lin Feng made the first move, stomping on the ground, causing it to shake and what followed was biomass spikes erupting from the ground, forcing Graves and Richard to leap into the air to avoid the lethal spikes. Su Xue targeted Richard, launching herself at him with incredible speed, aiming to push him further away and isolate their fight.
